address: C. de Marcenado, 34, Chamartín, 28002 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text:
I'm going to share an experience that happened to me years ago, and to this day it still bothers me because, at my age, I didn't have the tools to handle it properly. The disappointing thing is that the "professional" in charge didn't handle it well either.
I completed the leisure and recreation monitor course at Aula Joven (Youth Classroom) and suffered numerous instances of disrespect from one of the girls (who attended the course with her partner and was also getting her certification): intimidation, insults… she even threatened to kill me. Furthermore, this girl was working with them at the time and was considerably older than me (I think she was a skating instructor back then, with long hair and lots of braids…), which worries me a lot now that such an extremely aggressive person was working with them.
At the time, I was 15 or 16 years old, I don't remember exactly, and terrified, I told the person in charge after putting up with it for so long. She didn't take the appropriate measures because she continued to threaten me with death afterward. What's surprising is that sometimes I was paralyzed with fear and couldn't even move, and this person still didn't realize it was ongoing… I find it deplorable because I didn't have the courage to speak up again since they threatened to kill me if I did, on numerous occasions.
It could have been an unforgettable experience, but unfortunately, because of people like that, and those who are anything but responsible, I would never get another qualification from this company, nor would I ever send my children there in the future. They should be more careful with these things and not let anything slide because these aren't things to joke about or hesitate over. I hope they are now, because my comment is solely to ensure that something like this never happens to another child. I have the tools to deal with it now, but at that time and age, I was vulnerable and needed help. I asked for it, and it wasn't provided. I hope this never happens to another child, and if the person who did this reads this today, I hope they realize the kind of person they were. I hope they've changed and are now a good person, and certainly that they're no longer working with children. If the person who should have taken action but didn't reads this, I hope that if they continue working with children today, they do so with the care, respect, and protection that every child deserves.

I'm editing this review as a reply since there's no way to respond directly. Thank you very much for the prompt response, and I'm glad you now have a specialized team, but it's not fair to lie about something like this by saying the person was expelled, because that wasn't the case. I wish it were, but it's not true, so please correct what you said.
review_2_text: My son had a fantastic time! The counselors were incredibly patient and friendly. The setting was perfect, the food was great, and the activities were so much fun! Thank you so much for making this trip an unforgettable experience for my son 🙏
review_3_text: We wanted to express our gratitude for the wonderful experience our daughter had on this end-of-year trip. It will undoubtedly be unforgettable for her. We also want to thank the counselors who were so wonderful and dedicated, always looking out for the children's enjoyment and well-being. Thank you all so much. Happy Summer! Best regards.

If I could give it zero stars, I would.
Yesterday we dropped our children off at the Bakio (Bizkaia) camp, and in less than two hours we were already taking them out.
The facilities were in a deplorable state of safety and hygiene. We later learned that it was closed three years ago by the Provincial Council and the City Council, and supposedly it doesn't have the necessary license. It's outrageous that they play with our children's safety like this.
They aren't responding to our emails or our demand for a refund of the camp fee, given what turned out to be a scam.
Disgraceful. Don't send your children there!
review_2_text:
We booked a camp offered by Verano Aventura, located at C. de la Cruz, 22, Centro, 28012 Madrid, for our daughters from June 29th to July 6th, 2026 in Suances (Cantabria), and the experience was profoundly unsatisfactory.

The advertised program was not followed. The only activity outside of camp was a brief 40-minute surfing session, organized by the counselors themselves, who wore wetsuits in terrible condition. No transportation was provided at any time, forcing the children to walk for up to an hour and a half along the road, even in bad weather, exposing them to dangerous situations.

The food was clearly insufficient, to the point that our daughters had to buy additional food on their own. They returned home hungry, disappointed, and frustrated by an experience that should have been enriching.

We hereby inform you that we will be filing a formal complaint with the Consumer Protection Agency. We consider it unacceptable that Verano Aventura would take advantage of families' trust. We hope this situation does not repeat itself and that other families do not suffer the same disappointment.
review_3_text:
Shameful!!!
Today we took our daughter to Bakio, and many parents, after seeing the facilities, turned around and took their children home.
Dirty, in poor condition, dirty and torn mattresses, broken electrical outlets... we're going to file a complaint, but I hope the camp is closed for the sake of the children who were left there.

review_1_text: This is my third year, and my son still goes with enormous enthusiasm and comes back thrilled and happy, with a post-camp emotional hangover, as he says, for weeks afterward. The activities are varied and in close contact with nature: hiking trails and bivouacking, ropes courses, zip lines, and a climbing wall. No cell phones allowed. The facilities are excellent: a building with restrooms, a kitchen, and a dining area. Cabins for up to six people with bunk beds are available. There's also a private swimming pool.
review_2_text: My children have been going to Aventurarte camp for seven summers now. They love it; they make friends, it gives them independence, they're outdoors, they're constantly doing teamwork activities, and they don't use their phones except to call their parents on certain days at a specific time. As a mother, I think it gives them autonomy, and they have a fantastic time. The counselors are always on top of things and available to us if anything happens. The activities are great; they hike a lot in the mountains, sleep under the stars one night, and swim in natural pools. They also have a swimming pool and sleep in wooden cabins. review_1_text:
If anyone is responsible for skiing being one of my greatest passions today, and for allowing me to enjoy it on slopes of any difficulty, it's undoubtedly the Eduma team.

I was only 5 years old when I started going with them every Saturday to the mountains near Madrid. I was just a little boy with a terrible fear of skiing, "forced" to go by my parents because it was my grandfather's favorite sport. I still remember how Mari Carmen would pick me up crying from my parents' arms because I didn't want to go, and sit me next to her on the bus to calm me down. I also remember how Isra taught me to ski, holding me between his legs and encouraging me with: "COME ON, Super Fer!"

These are precious memories and moments that I will never forget. A clear example that when something is done with care, passion, and professionalism, the result is always excellent.
For all these reasons, I recommend and will always recommend that any parent, young person, or child try skiing with the Eduma family.
Hugs to all.

Super Fer 💙⛷️

review_2_text: My daughter loved the camp so much that she wants to go back next year. She made lots of friends and enjoyed the activities, including English. She also liked the food, and everything was very clean. I highly recommend it.
review_3_text:
Well, my opinion of the Madrid camp is that, after reading everything so positive, such detailed explanations about respecting the individuality and needs of each child, and a commitment to a daily log of activities and photos on a private blog, it turns out to have been quite disappointing in terms of a lot of misinformation. I understand the restriction of phone calls to just one day, but not that they wouldn't even let a child read with his reading lamp, knowing he had trouble sleeping, and without addressing or even being available to help him with his discomfort. There was no daily log, and after complaining about the scarcity of photos, which were difficult to find among hundreds of photos of all the children's groups mixed together, there were a few more, but only from half the activities, including going to the river after telling the children they wouldn't be going. In Madrid, by the way, there aren't even cell phone numbers for the counselors to contact, names of the people in charge of your child, any further information about the group formation, or anything at all.

The English level was mediocre, and the counselors seemed more concerned with speaking better Spanish than with guiding the children to improve their English.
My son also enjoyed several much cheaper camps more than this one, given our expectations. What a shame 🥺🥴

review_3_text: Much to my regret, I must express my great disappointment with the "El Guijo" camp, run by the Vaughan group, as a "camp to practice and improve English." My daughter has just returned from camp. The counselors speak English, and the children only speak English when addressing the counselors. They don't practice English at any other time. Spanish is spoken everywhere. The children are grouped by age, not by English level. No hace calor!! Eso era algo que yo queria para que mi hijo disfrutara mas. Un clima super agradable en Julio es algo que cambia totalmente la experiencia de un campamento.

Pero lo que hace excepcional a 1001 Aventuras , es el personal que cuida a los chicos. Los cuidan y se preocupan por cada uno de ellos, no solo como grupo. Desde antes me ayudaron con que mi hijo se tenia que ir un dia antes. Jose Luis me lo llevo personalmente  a Orense a la estacion del Ave. Ademas alli surgio un problema serio por que me falto hacer un formulario especifico del acompañamiento de menor y el lo resolvio . Fue dificil pero mi hijo estaba totalmente tranquilo gracias a la actitud y actuación de Jose Luis.

Muchas gracias Jose Luis a ti, a tu hijo y a todo el personal de 1001 Aventuras por vuestra dedicación y cuidado de los chicos. A mi hijo le habéis dado un verano inolvidable!

review_1_text:
Highly recommended! My 4-year-old had a fantastic time last summer. Jesús answered every question I had without hesitation, and my son was delighted with the counselors who worked with him. He told me he loved going.

We'll definitely be back this year! Thank you to the whole team for taking such good care of our children.
review_2_text: Due to a work emergency, I had to drop my daughter off at camp almost overnight. They made the registration process incredibly easy, and she had such a great time that we'll definitely be going again this year.
review_3_text:
My sons, ages 3 and 6, have been here for five weeks and absolutely loved it. This was my oldest's second year, and although we were hesitant about whether or not to take my youngest to a sports-oriented camp this year, even though she's only 3 years old, after a conversation with Jesús (the camp manager), we decided to give it a try. She really enjoyed it and can't wait to return next year, just like her brother. The instructors are top-notch; they're very involved with the kids. My sons told us every day about the things they did with this or that instructor with a smile from ear to ear. At the pool, they pay special attention to the children. My sons struggle weekly at school on their pool day. Here, swimming has been offered daily, and they thoroughly enjoyed it without fear or stress.
As a point for improvement, and the reason I didn't give a 5-star rating, is the FOOD. My children had breakfast and lunch at camp. The menu, in my opinion, is designed to be easy for children to eat, using simple and basic foods, such as pasta, rice, sausages, and breaded chicken/fish, always finishing the week with hamburgers or pizza and ice cream...
This year my children have particularly complained that the food was cold, the rice and pasta chewy (I understand, very overcooked), and generally very bland. The oldest told me that if they asked for seconds, they usually wouldn't let them because there weren't any more, and that their hamburgers only had meat, but the instructors' burgers had everything...
For children who only go for 1 or 2 weeks, I don't think it's a problem, but my children have been here for 5 weeks and have gotten a little tired of the lunch and breakfasts, which were always the same.
It's true that with all the exercise they do, I'm not worried about the type of food—maybe it's a bit saturated with carbohydrates and sugars—but I am concerned about the quality and variety of it, only including vegetables and legumes once a week.
We'll still be back next year, because the most important thing, the way we treat the kids and the overall good experience, is worth it.
We hope the food improves. We'll try it next year, and if it's still the same, we'll look for another option, maybe eat out.
Thanks team, have a great year, see you next year.

review_1_text: My daughter returned today and is delighted with the camp. She keeps saying that she'd love to go back and that she already misses the counselors. The counselors were disrespectful, with constant threats, a lack of empathy, a lack of patience, sarcasm, constant shushing, and a complete lack of professionalism. review_3_text:

If it had been in accordance with their terms and conditions, we would never have signed our daughter up for this trip.
We've received both their emails and ours demanding our rights, and their approach to us as a family from the beginning has been very unprofessional and leaves much to be desired as a company.
They've refunded our money, leaving us owed €40, which, according to them, isn't a small amount for the bus. They claim that we're the ones who canceled, and we did cancel because they changed their rules once the trip was paid for. We know that seat will be filled, but they refuse to refund the money, which is ours. Seeing the other complaints in their review, it's no surprise they've given it a 3.6.
I've also found complaints filed against them from the OCU (Spanish Consumer Protection Agency) regarding the same issue with the bus fee. Luckily, these days, all you have to do is Google it.

As a quick summary, it's brilliant. If you want a family vacation with your children, look no further.

To start with, the Bierzo area is an incredible region, with true nature, forests, rivers—which have water where you can swim—and places where you can still see the stars in the night sky. Very close to Madrid but without the unbearable overcrowding of the Sierra. What can I say about the pleasure of sleeping without the heat and having peace and quiet?

As for the activities, it's a ten. You won't be bored for a moment, and you can be sure that the little ones will be entertained and well cared for: hiking, canoeing, visits to Ponferrada Castle, the Mining Museum, Las Médulas, zip lines, a winery, astronomy, theater... plus, some activities were just for kids and others for adults.

As for the team, it was also outstanding; absolutely all the instructors went out of their way to make the experience perfect. Special mention to Carlos for his kindness and friendliness, and the flexibility with which he adapted to the needs of the guests and the way he treated us throughout the week. Thank you so much for that amazing paella at the reservoir.

In conclusion, this was one of the best vacations I've ever had and an experience I'd definitely repeat. Plus, the price was super affordable.
review_2_text:
DO NOT TAKE THE MONITOR COURSE HERE!!!!!
I took the monitor course in the summer of 2023, and I didn't receive my certificate until 2025. Both the experience inside and outside of the course was horrible. I took the intensive theoretical course with them, and it ruined my summer. Long days of boring, impractical classes, very short breaks, and from time to time we did free work for their children's camp, like moving the hostel mattresses, which you can tell me what educational purpose that served. The teachers were terrible, and I didn't even like them as monitors either. The director, Carlos, was one of the worst leisure and free-time workers I've ever seen. He talked like I've never seen anyone do, and he wanted to give you life lessons when you asked him a simple question. His arrogance was always present in his speeches; even his classmates couldn't stand him. Even our parents disliked him. They were incredibly inflexible with the bureaucracy and generally incredibly unsympathetic. The email service was awful. They didn't respond for four or five months while they were processing our titles, and we received news that the company had gone bankrupt, and no one wanted to answer our questions about the title. We were on the verge of filing a complaint.
Overall, it was a terrible experience, and not because I'm a lazy person or unfamiliar with the camp world, because I have plenty of experience. No one could understand the incompetence of this company, not even the other bosses I've had, nor anyone I know in the OYTL world. Terrible, terrible, terrible.

review_1_text: We enjoyed the Villa de Madrid Olympic wrestling championship, and I have to say that the venue, besides being well-organized, is perfectly suited for this type of event. A great choice.
review_2_text:
I've just filed a complaint with the Community of Madrid, and they're still surprised. My daughter has mild cerebral palsy, which results in a generalized developmental delay. She's been attending GYMCAM for two consecutive years. The camp has been aware of my daughter's needs from the very beginning and has never given me any trouble. This year, she was registered for the last week of June and the first two weeks of July. Two days after the camp started, I received a call telling me, and I quote, "they DON'T HAVE STAFF FOR CHILDREN WITH SPECIAL NEEDS." They told me she could finish this week but that I should find a more suitable alternative for my daughter in July. You can imagine what this has meant for me, both logistically and personally.

Now they've changed their story and are saying that I was aware they don't have SUPPORT STAFF. Would anyone in their right mind think I would register my daughter for a camp that doesn't have any professionals? Given this attitude, which I wouldn't even know how to categorize, I hope all the parents who believe in inclusion and in the right of children with disabilities to fully enjoy all rights and freedoms on equal terms with other children will take note. If they lack the resources, knowledge, or sensitivity to work with children with special needs, they shouldn't offer the program, much less accept them in March only to dismiss them in July.
review_3_text: I don't recommend it. Too many children and not enough staff. Lots of rules because otherwise they can't control the children. They're overwhelmed. On top of that, the director is quite unfriendly. You can tell she's been there too long and is fed up.

review_2_text: My daughter just returned from the Alicante Nautical Camp, and although she had a great time, she won't be going back because the value for money is terrible: The facilities (10 days in a room that couldn't be ventilated because the windows were screwed shut). The nautical activities, despite being in Alicante, don't take place at sea but at the mouth of the Segura River (dirty and smelly). The food (10 days eating junk food, not a balanced diet). It's not a cheap camp, and I expected much more.
review_3_text:
A complete scam and terribly organized.

1. I arranged for my daughter to be picked up in Burgos, but they didn't show up because, according to them, the route didn't have the information. I had to take my daughter to find the bus myself.

2. They didn't drop my daughter off at her camp in Asturias because they forgot to call her and took her to Cantabria instead. I was the one trying to sort things out. Finally, they returned her to Asturias after a 10-hour journey. All of this happened without me receiving any calls or explanations from anyone in charge of the activity.

3. At the camp, it was supposed to have age-based separation. My 12-year-old daughter was with children between 9 and 16 years old, all mixed together.

4. The counselors, who I don't know if they were qualified or not, were 17 years old, inexperienced, and treated the kids very carelessly. 5. Such was their inexperience that they didn't know children need to be told what to do, and when they did some activities, more than one child was left behind at the hostel. There were also missed transportation requests due to arriving late...it was utter chaos.
6. Many of the proposed activities weren't carried out. You pay for misleading advertising. They consisted mainly of hikes, and without enthusiasm, because the atmosphere was clearly unfavorable due to the inexperience of the counselors.

7. Not to mention the complete lack of cleanliness, the poor food (pork loin for lunch, pork loin for dinner...every single day), the thefts, and the total neglect of the children, which led to more than one incident.

8. And of course, on the return trip, I arranged a pick-up location with my daughter's guardians, and it's a good thing I was involved again, because the route had no information whatsoever, and the others didn't want to leave her there until I had to intervene once more.

As the days go by, I'm discovering more about this deceptive, expensive camp...and given the circumstances, even dangerous.

I pass through here every day, and even though it's a basic station, it's quite well accessible. Although wheelchairs aren't allowed inside, elevators are being installed on both platforms.

Upon entering, you'll find the area to top up your transport card, the turnstiles, and the information desk.

The staff is very friendly and helpful.

The station is quite spacious, well-signposted, and accessible; one minor issue is that some trains with a small platform don't quite align with the platform level, but this could be improved.

Although the trains are older, they run like new. It's quite normal for the PA system to be muted or for the announcements not to be projected on the signs; this is quite normal since they are older trains and can occasionally malfunction.

Overall, it's pretty good, but accessibility could be improved. Otherwise, it's quite good.

Also, there's a supermarket at the entrance and a McDonald's right across the street.

DISAPPOINTING END-OF-YEAR TRIP TO SIERRA NEVADA
We booked this trip with great enthusiasm because it included ski lessons for several days, which was the main objective of the activity. However, the resort remained closed due to weather conditions, and while we understand that this is beyond the company's control, what has deeply disappointed us is the subsequent handling of the situation.

The students received no ski lessons whatsoever: one day they spent almost the entire time on the bus, another day they visited a museum, and on the last day the trip was brought forward. Despite this, the company has only offered a refund of the ski pass, refusing to refund the cost of the lessons that were never given.

We believe that the response has not been in line with what was agreed upon or the expectations we had, since the lessons were an essential part of the trip. We expected a fairer solution and greater consideration for the families.

We hope they improve their handling of incidents and customer service in these types of situations.

review_1_text: There are few places as incredible as this to celebrate a birthday: rock climbing, zip lining, archery, water games... and overnight camping! But all of that pales in comparison to the instructors. Daniel, Benja, and Javi entertained, cared for, and pampered the girls like I've never seen before! A 10/10 team! Thanks a million for a unique weekend!
review_2_text: This is our second week at this camp, and the disorganization at drop-off and pick-up was already noticeable the first week. Today, that lack of organization reached its peak when, while looking for my 3-year-old (who was supposed to sign in at 4:00 PM), we waited and waited, but he didn't come out. We asked again, and they shouted my son's name to each other and went back inside to look for him. When he still hadn't come out after several minutes, but other children did, I asked again (by now getting annoyed). Almost 15 minutes passed, and they brought out a girl to give her to "her mother," and the woman said, "This isn't my daughter." We're really starting to see the level of organization. For the fourth time, I asked for my son, and no one answered. Counselors went in and out, but nothing. I told them I was going to go in and get him myself. The coordinator came and told me, "None of the children are responding." What kind of answer is that? Isn't it their job to be able to identify the children? I'm not asking them to recognize everyone by their physical characteristics, but it wouldn't take much to write their name on their paper wristband with a marker, especially considering that a 3-year-old watching TV or playing might not be paying attention. Seeing that they were finally taking the little one out, I asked to speak with the coordinator, who wasn't rude at any point, but the word "coordination" carries with it a responsibility (especially organizational) that isn't being met here. She invited me several times to file a complaint and gave me an UNOFFICIAL complaint form, which isn't legal, and they should know that. It's ESSENTIAL that when dealing with large groups of children, we're organized (nobody wants to regret an incident), and I don't accept the excuse that "the monitors are too young" if they aren't qualified to handle the organizational needs in an environment with so many children. My recommendation is that they find another line of work. We parents are entrusting the most precious thing in our lives to a poorly organized group of "professionals" who, in my very particular opinion, are NOT QUALIFIED.
review_3_text: We visited a few days ago with a group of teenagers. It's a welcoming place, and we're especially grateful to Alberto and Borja, who made everything so easy for us. They have lots of animals, a very spacious area, and a ropes course that our kids really enjoyed.

review_2_text:
Un lugar magnífico para aprender rodeados de amigos y diversión. Voy a explicar un poco mi experiencia por si os estáis pensando en apuntar a vuestros peques.
Mi hija tiene 13 años pero ya se ha venido aquí desde bien renacuaja, más o menos 9 añitos o así.

Siempre lo goza a tope y se le pasa volando de lo bien que lo pasó oiste.
Si no sabes si apuntar a tus hijos ni lo dudes que siempre lo pasan chupi.
Mi peque hizo muchos amigos y siempre vuelve a casa tope de contenta diciendome anecdotas y diversión. Es un sitio con muchas buenas vibras, aprendizaje, fácil de aprender, lo pasan tremendo

Oiste cariño, mi hija es muy de brazos abiertos, el primer día que había nuevos ya se les acercaba de brazos abiertos a conocerles y saludarles y siempre hay gente como mi peque muy social y muy abierta que reciben a los nuevos así de bien. Así que no te preocupes cariño que siempre lo pasan piruli con sus nuevos compañeros y les acogen muy bien.

Si te ha resultado util mi reseña puedes darle like o megusta para ayudar a que la lea mas gente. Gracias cariño y gracias por leer hasta aqui, gracias a los monitores que le habéis devolvido la felicidad y la sonrisa en la cara a mi peque que lo habíamos pasado muy mal cariño pero gracias de corazón por ayudarnos y hacer que mi Lauri vuelva a casa feliz y con risas de nuevo

Precio un poco alto eso lo unico malo que tiene pero ahorrando para ver feliz a mis peques lo mejor que puedo hacer, te lo recomeindo que vale la pena ahorrar por nuestros amores que nos devuelven la vida por lo menos a mi ajjaj que mis niños lo son todo.

Un beso cariño muak! 😘😘

Ay perdón que se me había olvidado decir a que venía mi Lauri. Pues verás estaba apuntada a ginasia ritmica y natacion con su prima Patri, oiste. Tuvimos que desapuntarla un tiempo por la pandemia por la crisis y falta de dinero en la familia cariño pero ahorrando y trabajando duro de sol a sol todo se consigue todo sea por ver feliz a nuestros peques, pronto vuelve mi Lauri que ganas cariño de verte nadar y actuar otra vez campeona 😘😘

Yo si que tengo estudios y soy una persona que le interesa aprender y saber, pero verás cariño las circunstancías son las que te trae la vida y hay veces que te quedas en la calle sin nada, otras tienes suerte y te acoge algun amigo o conocido. Por eso cariño es importante conservar amistades y estar cuando lo necesiten, por suerte para mi yo he recibido ayuda de mis amigos y seres queridos y eso demuestra que si están en las malas son los que valen la pena. Ahora la situación pinta mejor y estamos remontando mi familia pero hay secuelas oiste. Siempre intenta aprender, nunca es suficiente, si puedes estudiar no lo desaproveches y lee lo más que puedas. Para ver películas y estar con el móvil siempre hay tiempo, pero eso no es lo que te va a dar tu  futuro así que piensa bien en que quieres gastar tu tiempo cariño.
Empecé dando una opinión y termino dando una lección de vida oiste jajaj aunque no creo que nadie lea todo esto hasta aquí así que si estás leyendo esto dejame decirte que gracias cariño y ponte a hacer algo por tu futuro siempre que tengas oportunidad!

review_1_text: I wanted to publicly thank everyone for the care my daughter received at the summer day camp in Santa Teresa de Calcuta. On the last day, just as we were about to leave, my daughter had an accident in which she fractured her wrist and subsequently had to put it in a cast. I must emphasize that both the counselors on duty and Laura, the nurse, attended to my daughter immediately. I don't know this because I was told about it... I know it because I was at the gate waiting for her to come out and I saw everything happen. Thank you to Daniel and especially to Lucía for her coordination, and to the counselors who made this unbearable heat bearable. Thank you for all your effort and dedication to these children.
review_2_text: Complete incompetence and shameful organization. I've been waiting six months for my son to be accepted into an after-school program. Back in September, they told me there wouldn't be a problem, that they'd call me soon. They keep changing the coordinator and can't even honor the previous one's commitments. In January, they told me there was an opening, and when I sent them my son's information (within five minutes), they said it was a mistake and that they'd call me as soon as they had a spot. I'm still waiting... I hope the school's PTA doesn't renew this company's contract.
review_3_text:
This weekend, they were in charge of a tournament. The first thing you need to know are the rules. In this case, I'm talking about futsal, where a youth team was eliminated despite winning and drawing both of their matches. The team we drew with qualified because they were counted as 3-0 after the third-place team in the group was expelled for misconduct. The score against them, before their expulsion, was 5-3 in our favor. The rules stipulate that in the event of disqualification, all matches against the disqualified team must be counted as 3-0, so as not to disadvantage any of the opponents.
In this case, that wasn't the case; the rule wasn't applied, and therefore the team that tied with us and didn't play with the disqualified team qualified. The organizers admitted their mistake with great empathy, but didn't correct it, so our team, even though they knew it wasn't fair, didn't make it to the finals.
They taught 11- and 12-year-olds that whoever speaks the truth and does what is fair always loses. For a company that also deals with children, I don't think they're the most suitable, as they don't convey good values. If we adults had started a conflict over that decision, it would surely have been different.
It's a shame that you take public money for things you don't know how to manage, not for the workers who are there all weekend, but for those who organize it and actually take the money.

review_1_text: My son spent three weeks there, and even though he didn’t knowSpanish he absolutely loved it. He came back stronger, both physically and mentally. review_2_text: My son went for a week; it's his first year. A unique and highly recommended experience. He'll definitely be back next year, and for a longer stay. Thanks to the whole team; your work is incredible. Have a great summer.
review_3_text: A unique camp where they teach the importance of teamwork. The counselors are professionals who never hesitate to help any child in need, and they're always available even when they're not at camp. They also organize extra activities outside of camp for anyone who wants to participate. My son has been going for three years and he likes it more every day. It's not a place where anyone is mistreated; on the contrary, if someone bullies, they're expelled. But they also don't coddle your child and read them a story. It's a shame they don't do something like this for parents. It's a fantastic team. Thank you, Great Captain!

The English course met my expectations, and the organization was impeccable. I felt supported and cared for at all times, which gave me a lot of confidence.

I wholeheartedly recommend ISL. Not only because everything is so well organized, but because they make the experience human, personal, and truly transformative. I would definitely do it again.

Volunteering with Pueblo Inglés is a wonderful, life changing experience.  I have volunteered a few times, and although the days can be long and demanding, the rewards are numerous.  Getting to know the Learners (usually Spaniards) is amazing.  It's an opportunity to get to know the people and culture at a much deeper level.  In addition, Pueblo Inglés organizes some activities that introduce different aspects of Spanish culture into the program.
I have volunteered at multiple locations and each offers its own unique character and charm.
This is a volunteer opportunity you will never forget and if you're lucky, you'll even make some new friends from around the globe.
review_2_text:
An experience of a lifetime!
The program is not only enriching academically but it greatly helps with social skills and self confidence. Pueblo Ingles process is easy and runs smoothly. Communication is great and friendly.  You’ll get all the information you need regarding logistics as well as what the expectations are from the beginning way before the start of the program.  Locations are fantastic in beautiful settings with delicious food , great accommodations and filled with activities. Make sure you bring a big smile and are ready to meat people from all over the world.  Monitors are awesome at creating a safe and fun environment for all. My raising junior has been a volunteer for two years and he is already looking forward to registering in January for Summer 2025. It’s been a life changer for him and I couldn’t been happier. Thank you to the entire team but specially to the two Ana’s who are the minds and arms behind the logistics to ensure the entire process is flawless. Hasta pronto!
review_3_text: My daughter had a great experience on her trip to Spain with Pueblo Inglés. The camp leaders were extremely kind & welcoming. The camp was well organised and activities were fun. The accommodation & food were excellent. She wants to go back again next year!

review_3_text:
NO ANTI-BULLYING PROTOCOL.
Response:
I'll be more concise.
There was BULLYING, YES
Were MEASURES TAKEN, NO
We decided our son should leave, YES. Obviously, he wasn't going to stay with the bullies.
You identified yourself, NO. He asked us to come look for him in a call, and while we were there, he told me what was happening.
Was there any interest from his superiors after the BULLYING, NO.
The supervisors supported him by informing him and then asked questions, YES.

I find it insulting that you're telling me he's vindictive when all I'm doing is stating the facts.

It would have been more humble of you to apologize and, above all, to take measures to prevent it from happening again.

THERE WAS NO ANTI-BULLYING PROTOCOL.

My 6-year-old son was bullied by some older children. No one noticed. The counselors' response was supportive, but our son was the one who had to leave the camp because no action was taken against the bullies, and no one from Abancay called us afterward to inquire.
The counselors weren't trained to identify it, and the managers weren't very good at making decisions.
It's a shame, since I went to some camps when I was little.
I hope these comments will help spur action.

review_1_text: We reached out to Sinead months before we relocated from the USA to Spain. Thanks to her guidance, she made us aware of several resources available for special needs children in Madrid. She really knows a lot about the schools and processes which differ greatly from the US. Patience is key when dealing with Spanish bureaucracy and she forewarned us. Shortly after arrival, we engaged Sinead to help us communicate effectively with the local education department and government officials. She attended meetings with us and communicated with them continuously on our behalf. While we had an unusual situation where it took the government agency several months to approve our daughter for school placement, Sinead did not let up and maintained constant contact with us and them. Sinead really is a wonderful advocate for families and children. She is responsive, knowledgeable and a lovely person. We definitely would work with her again.
review_2_text: We are so glad we found Sinead. We were planning to move from London to Madrid over the summer with our two daughters, and we knew that finding the right school was key to the success of the relocation plan. Even though I was born in Spain and I speak the language I was very worried as navigating the education system and application process can be daunting- not to mention having to do that while planning all other aspects of an international move! Working with Sinead was brilliant, we felt supported and reassured the whole way and her help was priceless: from doing the research, getting the expert insights, to visiting the schools and handling all the paperwork and deadlines. After that, the transition went smoothly. Two months into the school year, the girls are settled and happy :) Massive thanks!
review_3_text: Sinead was essential is planning our gap year in Madrid. Our 2 boys having a smooth transition was our biggest concern and we wanted to find a school that could help with that. She was great at explaining the whole process and type of schools, the dates that were important, but also more importantly putting us at ease with the decision making. She really listened to what would be best for our kids and helped find a school to align with that as well as get them registered. We did it all remotely from the states and got most everything on our wish list. We now live in Madrid, in a great family friendly neighborhood and are walking distance to our small bilingual school of choice. Thank you Sinead for helping make this happen.

review_1_text:
Dear students, let me share with you my amazing experience of Madrid and Canterbury TEFL. Madrid itself is a fascinating place, with many wonderful art galleries, museums, parks, great cafes, also a wonderful history.   There are many street processions, festivals, even people in fancy dress, and a strange cinema where you can see films with Spanish subtitles. All this is aside from the usual things in capital cities, such as shops, theatres, opera, and more Spanish cinema without subtitles.

Now to a particularly special part of Madrid, Canterbury TEFL. This is a VERY important experience for those of us who aspire to teach TEFL. On the Canterbury TEFL course, you will meet John Bouse, a person who is an expert in grammar, and can tell you everything about the English language which you always needed to know, although you never knew who to ask. My eyes were opened on the Canterbury TEFL course, by John, so that what once seemed incomprehensible became understandable. Although I already had a CELTA, I wanted to go to Spain, so I knew that the Canterbury TEFL course was the right place to start my Spanish job search and update my TEFL skills. Since then I have met and taught many people, having finally, through John's influence, become a decent teacher, which I originally was not. Canterbury TEFL is a valuable, desirable experience for those of us who wish to teach in Spain. I highly recommend this course, their staff, and obviously Madrid itself as a place which is full of interest, even full of people who want to learn English!

Spanish people often go to a bi-lingual school, but top up their English education through extra tuition like the type provided by Canterbury Consulting es. You may even come across professionals who simply wish to keep up their proficiency in English by talking to a native speaker. One of my students was a doctor, another a professional chemist.   However I also tutored a number of adults working for a solar power company as well as several children, varying in age from six to sixteen.  Some students are a beginner, but as always there is some understanding of English gleaned perhaps through school, even gained from a helpful parent. In Spain, children begin their languages education young. Canterbury TEFL does have a useful library of books and computerised resources which will be helpful to you as a student and potential teacher.

Many times I went out to taste Madrid night life with an adult student or a fellow tutor.  Canterbury TEFL is a great TEFL experience, Madrid is a fabulous place to start your TEFL career.
review_2_text:
I am so grateful that I took the course in Canterbury Consulting Spain in 2014 with our very helpful, kind and expert teacher John Bouse and James in the admin. My life has totally changed, my job and my environment becomes better since I finished the course. I was offered immediately a job after course then another open doors to work in schools and I am very happy where I am right now as I am working with children.

If you are looking to get a TEFL certificate please don't hesitate to take it in Canterbury Consulting Spain they are very helpful, supportive and effective. Your money will not be wasted as you learn, refresh your grammar, improve the language for bilingual teachers and you can immediately gain experience. Thank you very much Canterbury Consulting Spain.
review_3_text:
If you are considering teaching English in Spain, you have come to the right place with the Canterbury TEFL program.

I arrived in Spain in September 2011 and taught English with Canterbury for over a year. Especially for someone coming from the United States, Canterbury makes moving to Spain extremely easy. Whether you are worried about your visa, finding an apartment, meeting new friends, or the language barrier, Canterbury has you covered. The staff and teachers are always willing to help new students in the TEFL program feel welcome in Madrid.

No matter your command of the English language, I promise that you will learn something new in your four week TEFL certification program. Once you are finished and earn your TEFL certificate, you are guaranteed to immediately start teaching classes and earning money. You can also rely on the wealth of resources available at the Canterbury offices to help you prepare for classes with your new students.

Canterbury makes life outside of the classroom fun as well with a non-stop calendar of social events. Every month there are pub nights, field trips, and other exciting group activities that will allow you to get to know your fellow teachers better and experience many of the great things that Spain has to offer. If you want to move to Spain but are hesitant to make the leap, the Canterbury TEFL class is the way to do it

review_1_text: We love La Casita! Everyone is wonderful and our daughter really has a great time there. It’s a beautiful little casita with an outside garden and different rooms for activities. For our first time leaving our little for a few hours at school it has been a really nice experience!
review_2_text: We’re delighted with the fact that’s it’s completely in English and that the groups are very small and therefore receive a lot of attention. We previously sent our son and this year we’ve sent our daughter. Couldn’t be happier!
review_3_text:
Our child has been at La Casita de Inglés in Sanchinarro for eight years, and we couldn't be more grateful. They've learned a tremendous amount of English, but above all, they've enjoyed themselves, playing and having a great time from day one.

The Home Circle format is ideal: two hours in which the children rotate through the different areas of the little house, with native-speaking teachers and well-designed activities. Each week they work on a different theme, which keeps them motivated and eager to return.

The space itself is a key part of the experience: it's a real little house, like a cabin, with several classrooms and outdoor areas, making learning even more dynamic and fun.

We've also participated in summer camps and weekend workshops, and the experience has always been fantastic, with the same care and organization.

The learning is completely natural, based on play and group fun, and the respect for the children and their individual pace is evident. Both the teachers and the managers, María and Laura, are fantastic: friendly, professional, and always willing to help. They are excellent with families, very flexible, even making up missed classes, which is greatly appreciated.

It's a place where children learn English almost without realizing it and have a great time. 100% recommended.

review_1_text: I joined here with zero knowledge of Spanish. My teacher, Amaya, laid a strong foundation in the language for me. I hadn't informed her about how much I struggled with the language in my daily life, but she was perceptive enough to identify my needs and taught me all the basics required to survive here in a well-structured manner. Alongside the language, she also introduced us to Spanish culture. I greatly admire her dedication to her profession. She ensured that the classes were never boring, and her enthusiasm motivated me to listen more attentively. Beyond the classroom, she assisted me with notes and homework, doing her best to help me understand the language. I want to use this forum to thank her for her efforts, and I look forward to having more classes with her
review_2_text:
I joined Newsland Idiomas for a Spanish class with no prior knowledge of the language. I took a live classroom session with an instructor named Amaya, who taught Spanish in a well-structured manner. She emphasized the proper use of grammar and conducted numerous conversational classes, which enabled me to speak Spanish for the first time.

As a newcomer to Spain and the Spanish language, Amaya's sessions have been incredibly helpful for navigating daily routines and adapting to life in Spain. Learning with Newsland Idiomas felt like going back to my school days, making the experience enjoyable.
review_3_text: We are delighted with Newsland. My son has significantly improved his English, especially in listening and speaking. The classes are dynamic, the teachers are very professional, and their genuine interest in each student's progress is evident. 100% recommended.

review_1_text: A very positive experience at this academy. I especially want to highlight the attention and support I received throughout my time here: they have always been friendly, professional, and attentive to the students' needs. The quality of the classes is excellent—dynamic and well-structured, making learning much more effective and enjoyable. Without a doubt, this academy is highly recommended.
review_2_text:
I contacted this academy because I was interested in online TOEFL iBT preparation classes. When we spoke, we agreed on prices, and I gave them my timeframe, which was about a month and a half, and my target score.
The price is what you'd expect for classes with small groups and flexible schedules.
Most of the classes I took were with Mark, although on days when there were more than eight of us, for example, several of us would go to another call with a different teacher for more personalized practice.
Because the groups were small and the teachers were native speakers who knew how to teach well, the learning was quite solid and efficient. Since I achieved the score I needed, and the materials and Mark's willingness to answer questions were very helpful, I highly recommend Ashton Campion to anyone who needs this type of certification and is comfortable with online classes.
review_3_text:
Many thanks to all the professionals at Ashton Campion. I chose the academy for my children, and I can say it was a great choice. They've been there, and all three successfully passed the Advanced Level exam, the youngest just last year. It's been crucial for the older children in their current jobs. Flexible hours, online classes available... It's also an exam center.
I will continue to recommend you.
Many thanks to Carmen, Elena, Vanessa, and all the teachers.

review_1_text:
This is by far the worst gym I have ever used, and I would not recommend it to anyone - especially not people that aren’t spanish citizens.

My Danish friends and I were members of this gym for four months during our exchange semester in Madrid, and the service from the staff was consistently awful. Whenever we asked for help, employees had a very poor attitude and would even roll their eyes at us.

The worst experience happened when I tried to cancel my membership. In early December I went to the gym in person to cancel as I was returning to my home country, Denmark, permanently. The employee told me multiple times that my membership had been successfully cancelled.

However, in early January I received a message saying I had a pending payment. I contacted the gym to explain that this was a mistake, as I had already cancelled in person. They replied that because I didn’t have a cancellation receipt, they could not remove the charge. I was never informed that a receipt was necessary, and the employee clearly failed to process the cancellation correctly.

After a long and exhausting back-and-forth, they still refused to cancel the charge, so I eventually agreed to pay just to end the situation. But then I discovered that their website does not work outside Spain, meaning I couldn’t even make the payment. When I contacted them again, they told me they could not help and that I would have to return to Spain to pay in person - which is completely unreasonable.

I don’t understand why I should be charged for an employee’s mistake, and the lack of willingness to help resolve the issue is extremely unprofessional. This has been a very frustrating experience, and I strongly advise others to avoid this gym.
review_2_text:
€5 euro day pass to the gym, bring ID and a padlock for locker. Helpful staff at reception.
No smith machine but plenty of squat racks.
The main weights room DB rack tops out at 35kg and gets very busy so be ready to swap exercises or, heaven forbid, speak to a local in your best Spanglish.
Top floor is open to the sun, has dumbbells, free weights, squat rack and a few other bits and pieces, including a pointless jogging track which is too small to do sprints.
The swimming pool is 25m and always full of people, I would not swim there.
review_3_text: I was in Madrid for 4 days and I really enjoyed going to this facility. For anyone curious, it costs 5 Euros per entry and you need to present ID. The pool is clean and has various lanes for different speeds. You need a swimming cap to swim but they sell them for 6.5 Euros. The gym has everything you could need for a workout or cardio session. It would be even better if the staff were more friendly at the reception, but it's still a good and affordable facility to use.

review_2_text: For the neighbors, it's a real nightmare. They often finish playing at 11 pm during the week, and they don't let anyone rest on weekends either; they have no respect and you can always hear them shouting at the top of their lungs.
review_3_text: The club's history is a farce; what Google shows is a complete lie. It was founded in 1974 by Félix... from Félix's bar. Around 1979, the Santa Ana neighborhood was established, and the Santa Ana residents' association was founded with Alfredo Fernández Díaz, "El Ruso," as its president and founder, along with two other gentlemen whose names I don't know. The Santa Ana residents' association founded its own football team so that the kids wouldn't be on the streets with drugs, etc. Since there was already another Santa Ana team, they decided to merge them; hence the team's name and the logo that the founders designed for the association, which is still used by the team today. Years later, a man named Rafa, who owned a car dealership, became its first and main sponsor, along with Alcampo (La Vaguada), Coca-Cola, the butcher shop, the poultry shop, and the fishmonger, all thanks to the invaluable help of the neighbors. This is the true story of the team, not the one this guy omits or distorts in case someone interested wants to search for it on Google.

Guys, we were unlucky today because  we weren't allowed to enter! 🤭
So, if you want to go swimming here or at any pool in Madrid, you need to book yourselves and pay the entrance fee online or in cash @their respective office.

Using Google app, visit "deportes web" and select where you want to go. In our case, we selected this place, and then made our booking on our desired date and for a number of people; then, confirm.  No need to register...because at the end of booking, QR code(s) shall be sent to your designated email address.
Soon, we will enjoy our swimming party with family!😄👍

That was another learning experience for us then....😁😁😁

Finally, we had our swimming here on Monday, Aug 16, 2021. We entered at 11am knowing that we will be staying here the whole day....but, unfortunately, one of the staff went around the place a quarter before 2pm informing everyone that we have to leave at 2pm because they are going to disinfect the place.  Later, we found out after reviewing their website that our reservation was only good for a half-day stay!OMG😪😅

So guys, now we understand that this is now our new life; our "new normal life"! 👋👍💥 Happy summertime!😘

March 2023, Visiting from the United States. Well. I had a great time here using the piscina cubierto(indoor pool)
1. You have to download the Madrid Municipal app, Madrid Movil, so you can reserve and pay for swimming in the indoor pool. Reservations are for 1hr increments. Your "ticket" has a QR code that they scan at desk inside the entrance. Semi-pro tip: Screen shot your ticket so you pull it up on your phone quickly for the staff.
2. Respect their rules. Show up on time, wear a swim cap, wear deck sandals in the locker room, vestibulo maculino for the dudes, to your freeswim lane, calle nado libre. Due to the number of lanes available, then you may have to circle-swim with others. Sometimes other lanes are occupied with classes.
3. The pool temperature was reasonable, near 80°F. I like it a little cooler in general and definitely if swimming with a cap. The pool has large glass windows and doesn't smell like an indoor pool. That's a plus.
4. I wish there was a pace clock, but given the people I was swimming with, I couldn't get up to full speed. That was fine.
5. The locker rooms were open, well lit, and clean with lockers to stow your personal belongings. Bring a small lock though.
6. Both the staff and the other participants at the facility were super friendly and helpful especially given my still working on my  "No hablo Espanol" skill level.

Like life, once you suffer through the first time, then each time afterwards gets easier.

Book a flight to Madrid from the U.S., and enjoy their hospitality.
review_3_text:
The outdoor pools are a great activity with kids for a hot July afternoon (Monday, 2024). There are 2 large pools with a shallow and deep end (deepest is 2.65m) and a kids pool. Nice setting with mature trees. Relatively quiet when we arrived but got busier after about 17:00 (still, not too busy to enjoy).

Some of the reviews here are from the covid era I think. We got tickets pretty easily when we arrived at 16:00 which allowed us to stay from 16:00 to 21:00 (no strict hourly slots like others mentioned). 1 adult, 2 kids for €4.95 total!! Staff reasonably friendly.

The 2 large pools were a perfect temp and jumping/diving is allowed (although no flips, etc). No slides, no diving boards. There are some sunbathing areas around the pool. The upper pool has the best shaded spots (there is a second large pool and a kids pool up some steps to the left where you enter - I'm sure some people miss that!). Bring a picnic blanket.

Most of your bags and stuff can be left around the pools, there are blue pouches in the changing rooms which can be used for phones, cash, etc. (not sure of the cost of this).

review_1_text: Hello, I'm the father of a player on the Under-16 B team. The people are really nice, and the prices are good. We've heard that a father from our club, Puerta de Madrid, ordered a glass and went down to the stands to cause trouble. Apparently, the president of our club has a grudge against Rayo Majadahonda and has reported them. But what he doesn't realize is that he's not hurting Rayo Majadahonda, but rather the friendly people who run the bar, who have nothing to do with Rayo Majadahonda or the feud between our two clubs. As a parent at the Puerta de Madrid club, and unaware of the feud between our two clubs, I ask our president to withdraw the complaint. All it will do is harm the family that runs the bar, all because of one of our fathers who decided to take a glass and put it in the stands when it's prohibited, and who was also drunk. review_1_text:
A center specializing in STEM disciplines (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) where children and adolescents can reinforce and expand their scientific knowledge while developing technical and creative skills.

The curriculum covers ages 5 to 16 (and beyond), with a well-structured curriculum based on four main pillars: mechanics, programming, science, and robotics. Classes are dynamic, participatory, and full of experimentation, making learning fun and very practical.

Each session is supported by a large number of materials, kits, and resources that make understanding the concepts easier and more visual. It's true that this isn't a cheap extracurricular activity, but it truly offers great value for money: the investment in materials, facilities, and, above all, the quality of teaching is evident.

In addition to their programs at the center itself, they collaborate with schools by offering extracurricular activities and actively participate in Science Olympiads and technology fairs, which reinforces their commitment to scientific outreach and learning.

But, without a doubt, the best part is the staff: friendly, enthusiastic teachers with a tremendous ability to connect with their students. They instill passion for what they do and help the children learn while having fun and feeling part of something important.

In short, a very comprehensive school, with a solid methodology, an inspiring environment, and a staff that makes all the difference.
review_2_text:
This school is a balm for the needs of one of our children, who has always shown an innate curiosity for construction, electronics, robotics, and this field of work.

review_3_text:
- Good teachers, small class sizes, and a great willingness to answer questions.
- Many of those who get in have very high A levels, especially in the international program. They push you hard, but if you take it seriously and put in the hours, you usually get it.
- In general, they take into account the evaluations that the students give the teachers at the end of the year.
- The class schedule (8 a.m.-12 p.m. or 4 a.m.-8 a.m.) leaves you plenty of time to study.
- They have agreements and many places (even at good American universities) available for those who want to go on an exchange. Few Spanish universities, if any, offer such a wide range of international programs.
- A very good school in the electrical sector. Probably one of the best in Europe.

- A lot of pressure and a lack of free time once the course progresses a little. If you don't keep up with your subjects, you fail the midterms and it becomes quite difficult to pass.
- More expensive than a public school, although in the end it all depends on the value you place on the advantages offered and your ability to take advantage of/reap the benefits of that education.

review_1_text:
The Paradox of Burnt Bread: A Dish That Provokes and Challenges

In the vast universe of gastronomy, there are times when culinary creation ventures into unexpected and provocative territories. One example of this is the unusual dish of burnt bread with tomato, peas, and a disturbing final touch: human fingernails. This combination, far from being a simple kitchen error, seems designed to challenge our perception of food and the art of the table.

The bread, undeniably charred at the edges, offers a crunchy texture that, while perhaps unconventional, provides a smoky flavor that is curiously complemented by the freshness of the tomato. However, the presence of burnt bread evokes mixed feelings; on the one hand, it evokes the essence of rusticity, and on the other, it provokes a slight aversion due to its unappetizing appearance. This duality, undoubtedly, is a starting point for reflection.

The tomato, thickly sliced ​​and generously arranged on the bread, provides a vibrant contrast. Its sweetness, accentuated with a touch of salt and perhaps a drizzle of olive oil, attempts to balance the intensity of the char and offers a breath of freshness amidst the heavier flavors. The peas, tender and glistening, add a smooth texture and a subtle sweetness, even more essential to this dish.

However, the inclusion of human fingernails in the presentation is an extreme twist that will not go unnoticed. This element is, without a doubt, a glimpse of surrealism and provocation. At first, it may cause discomfort or unease, but the intention seems to be to make a statement about the meaning of food, challenging accepted norms and confronting us with the harshness of existence. This is a dish that, more than nourishing us, seeks to open a dialogue.

On a sensory level, the contrast between the flavors—the smokiness of the bread, the freshness of the tomato, and the tenderness of the peas—is intriguing. However, the visual impact of the fingernails disrupts the balance, creating a delicate dance between the sublime and the grotesque.

In short, burnt bread with tomato, peas, and human fingernails is a dish that transcends mere food. While it may be shocking and, for many, unacceptable, it invites reflection on the limits of creativity in the kitchen. It's an experiment that lingers in the memory of any diner and challenges our understanding of what gastronomy can be. Undoubtedly, it's a sensory journey not for everyone, but for some, it will represent undeniable artistic value.

review_1_text: I requested information about studying there as an international student; it was recommended to me and I received excellent service. The staff is friendly and the facilities are modern, spacious, and pleasant. And well located.
review_2_text:
I have a daughter who studies at UDIT, a school that boasts of being very "modern" and having state-of-the-art facilities. However, the reality inside the classrooms is quite different. Students have to endure temperatures exceeding 27 degrees Celsius (81 degrees Fahrenheit), without adequate ventilation.

Some classrooms have a small window that barely lets in any air, while others have none at all: just large panes of glass to let in light and a single door, whose main purpose becomes ventilating the classroom, since it draws in the cold air from the hallway—which is also used by visitors to the school. The result is a closed, hot, and unhealthy environment for spending hours in class.

And what's most worrying: air conditioning, which would be the most logical solution, only seems to be available to those who pay extra. When students ask to turn it on, the response they receive is as disappointing as it is unbecoming of an educational institution: "Take off your sweater." I find this disrespectful and inconsiderate to the students' well-being.

A truly modern school shouldn't focus solely on aesthetics or technology, but also on ensuring basic comfort and health. Hopefully, they will take action soon, because a suitable environment is also part of quality education.
review_3_text: I'm hoping they don't delete my review like they're doing with some students'. I'm the mother of a student, and I find it shameful that with what we pay, our children are roasting there in 30-degree heat. This means that teachers and students alike are all sick and constantly complaining, but of course, they're students and no one listens. If your excuse is that it's a smart building and it sets that temperature when it detects cold outside, then either fix it or install air conditioning. On top of the 30 degrees, you're adding the heat from the computers. Does anyone here think it's normal, Madam Director, that they have to leave the classroom every few minutes to get some fresh air? Obviously, the teachers suffer too. It's not a technology campus, either. Please, more investment is needed in the computers. They're all incredibly slow; the Spectrum was faster. I think it's a disgrace that they study everything on computers and have such pathetic equipment. The only good thing there is the teachers—all excellent, a 10 out of 10. Otherwise, it's deplorable. Please take action now, it's inhumane to keep them like this

review_2_text: Taking the bootcamp at ID Bootcamps was one of the best decisions I've made in my professional development. The program is very well structured, with a practical approach that truly prepares you for the real-world work environment. The instructors not only have extensive knowledge of their subject matter, but they are also always willing to help and support you throughout the process. Thanks to the bootcamp, I not only strengthened my web development skills, but I also learned to work in a team, face challenges with confidence, and build projects from scratch. review_3_text: The Data Science bootcamp is excellent. The instructors are experts in the field and explain it brilliantly, making the course very manageable. review_1_text: This bootcamp has changed my life. It's allowed me to significantly improve my programming skills. I've learned a ton of technologies I was completely unfamiliar with, all of which are totally applicable to real-world work. No more archaic languages ​​that ultimately become useless. It requires a lot of work, as the training is intensive, but it's absolutely worth it. Thanks to this training, I was able to pass the exams and land a new job with the working conditions I was looking for, allowing me to live where I want and enjoy time with my family. 100% recommended!
review_2_text:
I finished the course a few weeks ago, and I can't recommend the company enough. The process is tough, but very rewarding. The entire team, both staff and instructors, supports and guides you, making you feel at home from day one. I will always be grateful for their work ethic and the way they treat you.
You never feel alone, and that's vitally important in a learning process like this.
They also make everything easy, and you leave knowing you haven't wasted your time. Without a doubt, I highly recommend them.
review_3_text:
If you have NO idea about programming and have never touched a framework or written a line of code in your life, NEVER choose the full-time option. Chances are you won't finish it. I would recommend taking the part-time option if this is your first time with programming.

I'm only giving it two stars because I thought the way I treated some students was terrible. I remember that when I counted the challenges to get the percentage of how many practices I completed, they told me that not all challenges had the same value. What? If there are 100 challenges and I complete 50, that means I've completed 50% of them. Well, it seems that for some reason that's not the case for them; for them, I've completed less than 50% (it doesn't make any sense). Maybe it's just me not taking notes in math class.

Overall, I've learned something, but basically I have to take extra Udemy courses. That's why I recommend that you don't take the full-time option if you're starting from scratch, even if the teachers tell you it's fine because others have succeeded. Remember this: if you have no idea how to program and you choose the full-time option, even if you manage to pass, you'll have to spend a fortune on other courses to implement what you've learned.

Finally, I want to say that you don't need to spend the money you're going to spend on a Bootcamp when you can study the same thing for €500 in courses on Udemy, Platzi, or OpenBootcamp (the latter is free). Look for experiences on YouTube. Cheers!

review_1_text:
I stayed there with my scout group; the facilities and location were good. The only downside was perhaps the limited portions of food, and while the service was adequate, the owner was quite understanding regarding minor inconveniences.

Thanks!
review_2_text: My daughter went on a school trip to this farm, and she and the other children came back very disappointed. First of all, the pool wasn't ready on May 30th, with temperatures exceeding 35 degrees Celsius. They also didn't do the stargazing excursion. Apparently, the counselors weren't very nice to the children, constantly yelling at them like drill sergeants. There was very little shade, and the children didn't like the food either. The children also mentioned that the bathrooms weren't clean. The children have been looking forward to this trip for two years, hoping for an unforgettable day and night, and it turned into a disappointment. It's clear that the school shouldn't use this farm for these kinds of trips again unless they significantly improve. Don't bother telling me to delete or change this review, as you've done with other reviews, because it's going to be clearly stated here. You're playing with the most precious thing a family has: their children.
review_3_text:
After reading some recent reviews, I'm amazed. I've visited this farm in previous years and can say with complete certainty that the change has been impressive: the facilities are completely renovated and adapted to all kinds of needs.

What I highlight most as a teacher is the innovation in the activities for the children. They are always accompanied by instructors, who not only supervise them at all times but also offer varied and well-organized activities designed for all tastes. The staff has always been cordial, friendly, and very professional.

Honestly, I find it hard to understand some negative messages, especially when they come from people who weren't even present.

My experience this year has been very positive, and I greatly appreciate the effort and the progress the center has made. Keep up the good work! We will definitely come back next year.

Staff barely speaks english so be ready to struggle during the training part.
Once you get used to it, it will be much faster and so much fun!
Recommended!
review_3_text:
This adventure park is great for families and groups or friends in the mood for a fun and active time, surrounded by nature. A double hook system provides plenty of confidence even if you're not specially keen on hights, and moving between trees, of +10 meters high.
Very friendly and professional staff explains safety measures and climbing  technique.
Highly recomendable for active families!

address: Cam. de los Campamentos, 1, 28470 Cercedilla, Madrid, Spain
review_1_text: As a Globalcamp counselor, I couldn't be happier. What's happening at these facilities is pure magic. Year after year, more than 100 children return to spend 15 magical days at camp; they grow with us, and we learn from them. A place that year after year earns the reputation of being the best camp in the world.
review_2_text: We were involved in an Erasmus+ project with young people from all over Europe on music and dance at Colladito de Cercedilla this September and had a wonderful experience. Although the hostel is old inside, the location, the surroundings, and the activity rooms are very welcoming. The food was delicious, and the staff were also 10/10. Thank you so much, team. We'll be back!
review_3_text:
An older hostel in excellent condition. It features a main building with four floors offering single, double, and group rooms, numerous bathrooms, changing rooms, lounges, and multipurpose rooms.

There is also an area with five well-maintained wooden cabins, each accommodating up to 12 people in bunk beds and with its own bathroom.

Finally, the third facility is a spacious dining area in a large wooden cabin, complete with a kitchen, bright windows, and plenty of tables and chairs.

All of this is situated on a large plot of land with well-maintained native vegetation, a multi-sports court, and a treetop adventure course.

Wi-Fi and heating are available.

The staff is very friendly, professional, and attentive, and they take excellent care of children.

short_address: Paseo Boadilla del Monte, Km. 14,5, Brunete
review_1_text:
Nice school farm to visit with the family. Most school farms in the Madrid area only offer the possibility to visit with a large group or a school field trip. I love that at El Alamo they also open their amazing facilities to spend a day in a farm with the family. We got to experience the same kind of field trip my kids used to go to with their school but on a weekend.
They showed us the animals and we got to interact with them. The kids got to ride a horse. We made cookies and did grape stomping. Lunch is included. Nothing fancy but tasty and served family style so you get a chance to interact with the rest of the group visiting.
It was a great outing.
I’m not giving it 5 stars because the horse they picked to do the horse riding activity didn’t seem to be appropriate for that. From the beginning it was very nervous and discouraged a lot of people in the group from riding it. Maybe a more calm horse would be more suitable for this type of activity. Other than that we will definitely come back and try new themed days at the farm.
review_2_text:
Our daughter attended the external camp for three weeks and the internal camp for one week. It was her first time staying overnight.

At the start of the camp, she only spoke basic Spanish, but she learned a lot during her time there! She made many friends and loved the food they served!

The camp is located in a beautiful place with great facilities, plenty of space for kids to be outdoors, and in contact with nature. There is a pool and many shaded areas to help withstand the heat waves.

They offered a variety of activities, many of which were centered around the farm and the animals. The monitors and staff were very professional and kind to the kids, and they genuinely seemed to love working there.

We were staying in Brunete and were able to reach the camp by public bus every day.

If we can, we would love to repeat the experience in the coming years!
review_3_text: We visited recently and my daughter had so much fun- I would recommend  wholeheartedly that you visit.

review_1_text: We're fortunate to have such an incredible space in Alcalá as La Esgaravita. The day of the farm school visit is one of the most special days for the little ones, and here all their expectations are met, leaving them with a bigger smile than they had when they arrived and a wealth of stories to share. The pristine, rural setting enjoyed here—shaped by all the animals and their history, the Henares River, which surrounds the farm, bringing fresh, greenery with it—and the loving care with which the entire staff (kitchen, coordinators, instructors, maintenance) prepares for the day and takes care of the farm to ensure everything is perfect, makes it a wonderful experience for everyone who visits this place and turns it into a natural symbol of the city.
review_2_text:
We visited the farm school this weekend to enjoy the Christmas Cottage activity and we have nothing but praise. The place is truly magical: the views, the illuminated trees, and the entire Christmas atmosphere create a beautiful experience from the very first moment.
The children had a fantastic time. They weren't bored for a second: they made cookies, prepared their "dream bags," and thoroughly enjoyed decorating the animal enclosures. One of the moments that fascinated them most was the nighttime visit with flashlights to see the animals; a simple activity that made everything much more exciting for them.

In short, a fantastic experience, full of charm, very well organized, and highly recommended for families. We will definitely be back.
review_3_text: We found this place through a friend who recommended it to us for our excursions with the kids, and I honestly couldn't be happier. It was a great choice. The staff is very friendly with the kids; you can tell they love their jobs, and everyone was delighted. They offer very interesting workshops to connect children with nature and to help them learn about animals. They have everything they need, it's a pleasure! Finally, we had the chance to enjoy the canoes for a while, and it was super fun. I definitely recommend it!

My only slight criticism is that it would be REALLY helpful to put a big sign saying “This way to Burrodiversia!” at the entrance to the path to the farm. There is a granja escuela with a similar name 300m down the road, and the path to Burrodiversia isn’t exactly welcoming, with signs saying “Private hunting ground” all over the place.
review_2_text: It's been an incredible experience! My 5-year-old daughter is so excited because she loves animals. And the adults have enjoyed it a lot too. It's all been so personal and in such a lovely setting. The animals were fantastic, and there were so many activities for the children. I assure you they won't forget it. And we were surrounded by the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, with beautiful views.
Special mention to Vuelta and Chipirón, who are especially charming.
It was a very pleasant afternoon with Álvaro and his animals. We will return.
