review_1_text: I spent a weekend at this hostel with a group of children, and while the surroundings are beautiful, the experience wasn't entirely satisfactory. The staff were somewhat unfriendly at times, and I think certain situations could have been handled with more empathy and better communication. Furthermore, the heating wasn't turned up despite being in the mountains in the middle of November, which was uncomfortable for the children. The use of space was also limited, as the hostel was shared with several other groups, making it difficult to organize activities. Overall, I believe there are aspects that could be improved to offer a more welcoming and suitable experience, especially for groups of children.
review_2_text:
I'm giving it one star because I have to give it something! TERRIBLE FACILITIES!
Hygiene is nonexistent; they didn't clean the public areas or anything all weekend. The cleaning supplies that were visible were in poor condition. The dining room wasn't cleaned after meals, and the utensils were dirty. We had to return the glasses and cutlery as soon as they gave them to us. We ate on trays and reusable plastic plates.
The food was beyond bad. The staff was incredibly rude. They didn't even bother to serve the food, and when you asked for something, they were rude and contemptuous.
The facilities are completely unmaintained. As for safety, there were iron fences on the ground at the end of the cabins, with no signage, as if they were covering a hole or who knows what. The cabins are for 10 people, in a super, super small space. There's only one electrical outlet in the cabin, and the bathroom door doesn't close properly and has a hole in it. The shower was claustrophobic and didn't have a bath mat. There were two bunk beds with broken slats, no curtain on the window, and the curtain on the door was torn. On top of that, the door wouldn't close, and to make matters worse, they turned on the "heating" just a few hours after we arrived, and we were freezing. As for the comfort of the beds... absolutely unbearable. The mattresses were torn and dirty, and the sheets were old and ripped, barely providing any warmth. The blankets were very stiff and uncomfortable.
The location is beautiful because of its surroundings, but unfortunately, the staff and the facilities made it unbearable to stay there.
review_3_text:
We went with our students and it was a wonderful experience.
The instructors were phenomenal with the children.
The organization was fantastic.

review_1_text: Basic lodges located in a great place if you need a stop for the night. Very warm, even though it was freezing outside. review_1_text:
For us, it's like our second home. We've been lucky enough to enjoy several weekends there, with friends and as a couple. Despite the challenges of having many allergies, the last few times they've been incredibly attentive with the food. The weekend team is magnificent.

We hope they stay, as there's been a lot of staff turnover.

Thank you so much to each and every one of you for making our weekends so special.
review_2_text: I will never forget this place and will return whenever I can. Being in the middle of an oak forest is undoubtedly magical. And if you also have such a beautiful river nearby... Sloe berries for making pacharán, autumn to listen to the leaves rustling... But nothing compares to the people who run it. The staff who helped us (I'm part of the AMIA association group from San Sebastián de los Reyes), as well as the waitresses, the cooks, and the chef I met when I went in to congratulate them, were all wonderful. Their simplicity, their straightforwardness, their politeness, and their sensitivity surpassed even the calm, welcoming natural setting, filled with birdsong in the autumn. Even, as is often the case, it didn't rain to everyone's liking, and I noticed the cooks' tremendous concern and tried to put it in perspective, because this is normal in life. And we must also learn to accept complaints and understand them. For my part, I leave with admiration for the people who work at this public, not private, institution. The food was so good it tasted like home cooking, at the very least. It's a simple place, not luxurious, so the rooms are basic, but perfectly comfortable and adequate. A wonderful place and wonderful people.
review_3_text: A spacious place, ideal for resting after a hike or for starting one 🚶🏼 🥾, plus you can easily spend a night or two there. It has a large cafeteria.

short_address: C. de José Fentanes, 55, primero, Madrid
review_1_text: A wonderful day in nature. Ignacio was a great guy with lots of knowledge and a deep understanding of nature. We found many edible plants and mushrooms. His dog Ika was a wonderful addition for dog lovers like us. He doesn't accompany everytime Don't expect dinner as nature changes and you never know what you'll get. We shared crisps, olives, wine and beer before heading home. Fresh air, sunshine,  light exercise and learning!
review_2_text: We had fantastic day with Ignacio, he had amazing knowledge in his field and his enthusiasm for his subject was infectious. I would thoroughly recommend this for anyone
review_3_text:
I don't have enough words to express the wonderful experience we had yesterday with Ignacio on the Wild Walk through the Vall de Sant Daniel in Girona. To say that we walked among wild boar tracks, fed three pitroigs (a type of wild boar), learned home remedies, and tasted plants like wild mustard, mallow, calendula, and white carrots would be an understatement. It was an experience that goes beyond the senses. One of those adventures that touches your soul and stays with you for life.

It was a magical walk where we remembered the importance of gratitude, reverence, and asking permission of Mother Earth. It was filled with moments where we embraced the joy of living with great intensity. Moments where we reconnected with nature and remembered that it is part of us.

Ignacio is an excellent guide with extensive knowledge of the environment at all levels. Through her games and explanations, she brought us closer, in a fun, simple, and different way, to the details of the natural world around us that we often overlook. A million thanks for this fabulous experience that we will definitely repeat!

review_1_text: I went on a bike trip with my friends because they showed us how to ride bikes, and I followed my friends. It was super fun riding bikes, but after arriving at the nature classroom, I was tired, and we ate. It was great, but only half of the nature classroom.
review_2_text:
My son went to an activity, and when I got back, I found out they'd given a talk and then put on the TV. My son said it was incredibly boring; he was fed up with listening to what they were saying, and then watching a video without moving in front of a screen was just as boring for him.
My son was excited to do a workshop, but he didn't touch anything, didn't experience anything, didn't investigate anything. They just sat him down, gave him a talk, and showed him a video. You should rethink your approach and innovate because, for my son, it was
utterly boring.
The only worthwhile thing was the walk they took.

My children have been students at Molino de Las Letras for many years, attending instrument lessons, group classes, after-school programs, and summer camps.
Their philosophy of teaching through emotion, through sharing music to make it truly motivating for children, is their greatest strength, along with the fact that all the teachers are specialists in pedagogy as well as being musicians. We were fortunate to have a very good teacher, thanks to Susana, but the management's treatment has always been poor, something that has worsened in recent months with several episodes that show very poor treatment of children and parents. I hope the teachers do very well and improve their management and customer service.

review_1_text:
Jacqy's ​​school is one of those schools that goes largely unnoticed because it's located inside a chalet and because its owner isn't exactly one of those people who uses marketing. In fact, Jacqy Díaz is a highly respected professional with an international reputation, but one who keeps everything very quiet.

Her school also has the merit of having been built by Rafa, her partner, and herself, and they've put incredible care into it. In fact, they're improving it little by little every day (now they're even building a swimming pool).

It has a very spacious classroom with a large mirror and a beautiful mural, as well as a changing room, a bathroom, and air conditioning. It also has sliding doors that, once opened, open the classroom to the outside, which is very pleasant when the weather is nice.

Jacqy's ​​school has hosted many great artists who have also offered workshops, and performances have been held there in a very family-friendly atmosphere. The school is near a theater where they sometimes perform shows that require a stage.

As for transportation accessibility, the Cuatro Vientos train and metro station is very close.

Jacqy is a person with a heart of gold who is beloved by her neighbors for having selflessly helped on many occasions, not only by offering her art for free but also by working in other community service projects.

Her strongest point in terms of styles is oriental flamenco. If you take a stroll through her school, she will welcome you with open arms, but remember that there is much more to it than meets the eye, so don't rely on first impressions alone, because you may be pleasantly surprised when you least expect it. She also has very affordable prices and a wide range of courses.
review_2_text: A very welcoming and special family-run studio, perfect for beginners. Having started in ATS (Anatomy and Dance Theatre), I can only thank the teacher and the owner for this opportunity, as these disciplines are not commonly found in this area of ​​Madrid. Excellent service. Warm atmosphere and lively intensive courses and group performances. Highly recommended! 😋🌻
review_3_text: The room is fantastic. Natural light. Mirrors everywhere, and the owners are lovely. Ideal for classes, events, and workshops.

Well, I won't repeat it.
I don't recommend it at all.

review_1_text:
It's an incomparable theater school.

I've been to many in Madrid, and there's nothing like this one.
All the staff make you feel incredibly supported. It doesn't matter if you've done theater before or if it's your first time, because they cater to everyone's needs without any discrimination.

And the atmosphere is so welcoming; you feel very safe and learn a ton. Without a doubt, it's the perfect place to do theater or any other performing arts discipline. Its director, Marian, is amazing. She's definitely responsible for everything coming from a very creative, passionate, calm, safe, and inclusive place.
review_2_text: I'm thrilled with the On-Camera Acting course taught by Javier Marco. Javier is truly delightful, and the atmosphere is fantastic for both learning and enjoyment! The EXPRESSANDO team is incredibly friendly. I'll definitely be back!
review_3_text: I had a video reel done with Estudio Expresando. Marian, the studio director, was incredibly kind and helpful throughout the entire process. review_1_text: Espacio Teatro de la Vida, as its name suggests, is a place where life provides a space for theater as a fruitful form of expression. The actors' emotions are there, within reach of the skin and the eye. It's an original, open space where the unexpected can happen and where the necessary things can move you with small-scale plays and engaging performances. All this is guaranteed by a maestro who organizes everything with care and great creativity: Luis Sampedro. This vibrant space in the heart of Madrid is well worth a visit.
review_2_text: Teatro de la Vida is a very personal space created by Luis Sampedro, a professor, actor, and playwright. An intimate, pleasant, and charming space, where all the theatrical performances are imbued with that magic and closeness to the audience, making you feel at home. I've seen several shows: Viejas Pánico, J. y J., Leche de pantera, etc., and I've left delighted. I recommend it to everyone, because the prices are also very affordable for any budget.
review_3_text: It's a magical place; it's like experiencing theater in a very close and intimate way—a truly unique experience. Each performance is a fantastic story; you live it, you share it with the actors so close to you. Nothing I say can surpass the experience of attending a performance. You have to go and experience it. I highly recommend it.

address: C/ de Magallanes, 23, LOC, Chamberí, 28015 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text:
One of the best drama schools in Madrid. None of the strange and exclusionary methods imposed by other, more famous schools.

I say this because I've been enrolled in several drama schools in Madrid for a while now, purely for fun, but in my opinion, none were as professional, comprehensive, welcoming, and affordable as La Lavandería—something so important to consider given the current state of social relations and the economy.

I consider it, if not the best, starting with the director, the administrator, and the teachers. And I'm grateful to everyone involved because, thanks to their teaching and generosity, I've been able to get my start in advertising and film with award-winning Spanish directors.

"I should also mention that the school's director and playwright has been directing and producing her own scripts for years... with several highly successful theatrical productions (for adults and children) at the Luchana Theatres in Madrid."

Another reason I enrolled at the school is because I'm delighted with the students, who come from all ages, professions, and political leanings. A mix that's ultimately very difficult to achieve, but not (and this has been proven) for this school.

Finally, and sorry for this long post, it's the ideal place, as in my case (an introductory theater course), to have a great time, forget about work, and make friends. A more complete therapy is impossible!
review_2_text:
The school is fantastic; the introductory classes are a lot of fun and you learn a ton. They also teach you to be part of the entire creative process so you don't just end up reciting a text. The teachers are great; each one has their own style and they share their best qualities with you, making you want to attend every class possible.

But without a doubt, the best thing is the friendly, family atmosphere that Carmen and Marta create. 100% recommended.
review_3_text:
The way the teachers treat you and the training they provide is wonderful.

There's a great work environment and a strong sense of camaraderie from day one. The teachers are dedicated to your learning and help you every step of the way.

It's a very welcoming and, at the same time, highly educational school.

review_1_text: The Teatro Rey de Pikas is wonderful; this is the second year in a row we've celebrated our son's birthday there. They don't just offer a space for us to have a snack and blow out the candles afterward; it's a space filled with art, talent, laughter, and pure magic. The children have a blast, but I'd almost say the adults enjoy it just as much, if not more. I was so impressed by Pilar's kindness and the enthusiasm she exudes. This year we went to see Pepo Capel, and he left us speechless. Don't miss it; it's truly worth it.
review_2_text:
An absolutely spectacular experience at Teatro de Pikas. From the very first minute, the care and attention to detail are evident: Juan's staging of the show "Soltero y Entero" (Single and Whole), his energy, and his connection with the audience make the performance dynamic, fun, and highly professional.

The atmosphere is warm and welcoming, which is no accident. Pilar, the theater's owner, exudes a passion and dedication that shines through in everything: the way she treats you, the organization, and the quality of each performance. You can tell when a project is led by someone who truly believes in what they do.

If you're looking for something different, full of laughter, talent, and a special atmosphere, Teatro de Pikas is a sure bet. Highly recommended. I'll definitely be back! 🎭✨
review_3_text:
Very entertaining and fun to have a good time!! A very funny show with a touch of dark humor that couldn't be missed 🤭

He's a very artistic kid from Cádiz who's really working hard to make his mark in this world. Give him a chance, you won't regret it!! 👍🏻

Adf Foundation - A bunch of penny-pinchers.

I paid €150 for registration, and before the season even started, my son decided he didn't want to play on the team because he didn't get along with his teammates. They refused to refund the money, even though they hadn't mentioned their favorite motto, "Santa Rita, what's given can't be taken away," on the registration form. I sent two emails and they didn't reply, not even to defend their position. If it's not about them getting paid, they completely ignore you, which is what bothered me the most.

I'm done with it. However, I did manage to prevent a friend's son from joining the club for the 2026-2027 season.

€750 less in future income, and the list goes on. I'll do my job well.
review_2_text:
Without a doubt, the worst stadium in Madrid; it's full of thugs who spend their time insulting and threatening visiting teams. People who identify with the club's colors, drinking copious amounts of beer while watching the match, look like hooligans. The club has already been reported several times for racist acts, insults, and threats.
Besides that, the stadium is poorly maintained, and the changing rooms are filthy. It's shameful that they sell alcohol at a sports facility; as a result, it's a football ground where fights break out every weekend.
review_3_text: This place is a complete mess. Management, coaches, players… If you want a wholesome club where your child can play sports because you enjoy playing as a hobby, this isn't the place for you. Their management is absolutely terrible; to them, you're just a source of profit.

I'm starting an internship for a program called "Grupo Accede." I don't know if it's confidential to share this information, but I don't think there's any problem. The Faculty of Education building is somewhat old but very practical; the classrooms are modern and very useful for any type of class. I've only been here for a week and I'm quite happy with this Complutense University. All the professors are very kind and attentive. I like that the faculty is connected to the library and other buildings.

The only drawback I can add is that, with the metro construction, the buses that pass by are always full of people. What I hate most are the classroom chairs. It seems ridiculous, but I find them too uncomfortable. Please change this if possible.

Another minor drawback is the Wi-Fi. If you bring a laptop to work, you absolutely need it, since it's too difficult for me to get the password and everything related to the connection.

The good thing is that the classrooms have electrical outlets (some don't work) to charge your laptop or even your own phone.

Everything else is perfect. I would really appreciate it if you could improve the aforementioned. Thank you.
review_2_text: The living room/dining room/study is unbearably hot; you can't stay there for more than 10 minutes or you'll start to melt. Being there is like medieval torture.

review_1_text: Beautiful historical palace in the hearty of the Spanish capital with delicious food that unites spanish, french, and international cultures 😀🙌
review_2_text: Taking advantage of its open day, we were able to visit this French institution, founded in 1920 and inaugurated in 1928 at the University City in Madrid. Its aim is to promote bilateral and international artistic, cultural, and academic cooperation and exchanges. It's certainly interesting to learn about its history and visit its wonderful rooms, including the library and the interior terrace with its fabulous views. We'll return another time if we hear about a visit.
review_3_text:
A monumental building that houses a residence for students and/or young artists, scientists, and researchers of Hispanic culture.

Founded in 1935 by the French Hispanist Jean París, the building became a stronghold for both sides during the Battle of University City (November 1936) in the context of the Spanish Civil War. As a result, the building contains vestiges of those bloody clashes.
Currently, it offers cultural activities and open days to the general public.

review_1_text:
I think the name is a little pretentious when it is so inhospitable and uninviting even at the entrance.

We arrived today, having walked all the way from the center, not knowing that the exhibition of th Design festival closed early. We also are architecture enthusiasts and were hoping to catch a glimpse of the incredible structure.

The lady turns us around at the door and quite strictly. No comment.

I can understand it’s a private space and off limits. Just don’t put yourself on the „map“ as a design, architectural and „educational“ hotbed of Madrid if you’re so unwilling to even let two eager visitors experience the significant building.
review_2_text: I visited this site to attend the 25th Madrid Design Festival, which is free to attend. There are also conferences and events on offer, some free and others for a fee. I found it to be a place with a unique architectural expression, with great spatial richness, using metal elements and glass on the façade, with a very interesting solution. Due to its interior design, it's ideal for this type of exhibition-based event. I would visit it again whenever there are events of this nature because of how well it turned out to be... Thank you!
review_3_text: The building that houses the institution is as worthwhile as the exhibition itself. This is my second year visiting the Madrid Design Festival, and as always, it doesn't disappoint.

review_1_text: I'm referring specifically to the 2016 Benjamin tournament held last Saturday in San Fernando de Henares. The tournament was very well organized, with the schedule being meticulously followed. The various trophies awarded were also excellent. However, the venue fees were the most expensive of any I've attended, I couldn't find any information about the tiebreaker criteria in the qualifying groups, and, most importantly, the meager refreshments provided to the children—they only received a Prime soft drink.
review_2_text: A great tournament, well organized, following the schedule and timetable. A good atmosphere and an organization eager to do things right and improve to remain a leader in organizing children's tournaments.
review_3_text:
A spectacular tournament held this weekend in Valdilecha couldn't have been better organized. It's clear that there's great teamwork behind it.
All matches were on time, not a single delay, despite having to organize 25 teams with all their matches.
Spectacular atmosphere, constant music, PA entertainment, and a calendar of player photos.
Food was freshly prepared, not pre-cooked.
And a spectacular trophy ceremony with trophies for all the teams that made it to the finals.
Reasonable admission prices for everything offered, and the kids always had their favorite Prime to hydrate.
The kids, who are the most important thing in the end, were always treated kindly by the entire staff.
Looking forward to the next one so we can enjoy the best tournament in Madrid.

review_1_text: Director of Admission office is a kindly, helpful and friendly woman!  The teachers are close to the students and support.
review_2_text:
This opinion is sincere and personal. If you're looking for a safe school with exceptional academic standards and an unbeatable learning environment, don't hesitate, this is the school for you.
Choosing this school for my children has been one of the best decisions I've ever made, and I'll explain why:
1. The academic level is top-notch. They have highly trained teachers who are fully involved with students at every stage, ensuring that each child achieves their best. The attention is highly individualized, classes run in an orderly manner, and students work on practically everything in class, so your child returns home satisfied and calm. My son, now in secondary school, feels that his teachers teach and support him, and he quickly gains experience, skills, and knowledge with enthusiasm and poise. In high school, the student-teacher relationship is ideal. My daughter respects and admires her teachers, but at the same time, she feels protected and appreciated by them, so she attends school confident and happy. The results of the EvAU and the IB Diploma Program speak for themselves.
2. The school environment is wonderful. The school has a powerful Guidance Department staffed by top-notch professionals. They work from a preventive perspective, meaning from the first year on, they actively foster solidarity, empathy, and respect within the group, preventing the much-feared bullying from taking hold. As a double barrier, they seek the early detection of emerging behaviors that could harm the group's functioning and address them with tact, discretion, and determination. They foster student diversity and minimize disruptive behavior, which is very enriching for all children, who grow into adolescents/adults with highly developed emotional intelligence.
3. The facilities are safe and innovative, constantly evolving, tailored to the needs of the students, and used exclusively by their educational community. Therefore, students walk safely through the school, knowing they will only encounter members of their community. The entrances are open exclusively during school entrance and exit times, and are always monitored by multiple school staff. There is no vehicle traffic inside the school. They have managed to maintain a balance between modern, state-of-the-art facilities and a moderate school footprint, with the benefits this brings for its care, supervision, and enjoyment.
In short, BASE International School has successfully integrated new and innovative methodologies into the principles of the Free Institution of Education on which it was founded in 1962, which improve it daily. It is a pioneering, contemporary, dynamic, and international school whose history has maintained it as one of the best schools in Spain since its inception.
review_3_text: Terrible, not to say outrageous. We take the kids there to play soccer matches on Saturdays, and they lock the locker rooms and won't let them use them. The other day it looked like a swimming pool because of all the rain, and they had to change in the playground. If they're not going to provide the service the children need, they should stop hosting the school. They're shameless, and on top of that, they think they're above the people because it's located in the area it is. Be more humble and humane, especially with the children. You're sorely lacking in manners. Not to be recommended. There are places a thousand times better and with better education.

review_1_text:

You arrive at the designated location and no one helps you. When you manage to ask someone there, they have the kid/girl playing basketball for an hour and then tell them there are no spots available.
It's not right to "play" with people's time.
review_2_text: I'm a player and from my point of view it's a very good club and they have good facilities.
review_3_text:
I signed my son up for this summer's 2022 basketball camp. I paid the amount indicated for one week via bank transfer. Their advertisement stated that the camp would be for five weeks, but the last week, Monday the 25th, I found out from a friend that it wasn't going to take place.
It was impossible to contact them; they didn't answer the phone or respond to the emails I sent the week before asking for confirmation on whether or not the camp was going to take place, and to help me arrange a work-life balance.
And of course, to get a refund of the €125 I paid.
Total disorganization.
Total inattention.
I feel ignored and cheated by this club.

We did an excellent tour with my son and a friend of mine. We were there on Saturday to see the stadium and the museum. Unfortunately, we didn't see a game because Real was playing on Sunday. Amazing stadium, better than Real Madrit (in my opinion) with big gates and comfortable seats. The viewing is spectacular.
Greetings from AEK fans 🦅🟡⚫️
review_2_text:
An Unforgettable Experience for Any Football Fan! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I recently visited the Riyadh Air Metropolitano for the Atleti Tour & Museum, and it exceeded all expectations. Whether you are a die-hard Colchonero or just a sports fan, this is a must-do in Madrid.

The Highlights:

1) The Stadium: A stunning piece of modern architecture. Walking through the stands gives you a real sense of the incredible atmosphere that fills this place on match days.

2) The Tour: Extremely well-organized. We got to see everything from the pristine locker rooms (the player stalls are immaculate!) to the press room and the dugout.

3) Comfort & Cleanliness: I was genuinely surprised by how comfortable the stadium seats are! The entire facility is incredibly clean and well-maintained.

4) The Museum: This was the standout. It’s an immersive journey through Atlético’s history, filled with passion, trophies, and interactive exhibits that really tell the story of the club’s soul.

Overall, a professional, polished, and passionate experience. Highly recommended! 🔴⚪️
review_3_text: We saw Atletico Madrid vs Osasuna and it was such a great experience. The stadium itself its one of the best i have ever seen. Fantastic archiecture and very contemporary. Because it is elyptical you can comfortably see the match from every corner. Sound its also great and one can enjoy Atletico fans cheering theough the game. Hospitality deck is also fantastic. Plenty of shops and great restaurants. We managed to get to our seats within 10 from our cab stop wven though it was a full stadium of 70.000 people.

description: Modern planetarium with exhibitions, telescopes & a domed projection room.
address: Parque Tierno Galván, Av. del Planetario, 16, Arganzuela, 28045 Madrid, Spain
short_address: Planetario, Parque Tierno Galván, Av. del Planetario, 16, Madrid
emails_found: buzon@planetmad.es, castilloalameda@madrid.es, correo@ejemplo.com, dg.ambibliotecas@madrid.es, dmuseos@madrid.es, imprentamunicipal@madrid.es, mmacmdifusion@madrid.es, museodehistoria@madrid.es, museosanisidro@madrid.es, oficprotecciondatos@madrid.es, sanantonio@madrid.es
review_1_text:
We’ve been here a few times to attend conferences. Each time these events were NASA-hosted, which means we didn’t have to purchase tickets (sorry, I can’t help with providing details on that front).

This place is quite large with a fully stocked gift shop that my kids love as well as an interactive museum downstairs. The planetarium charges about €4 per person on the weekends to sit and watch the giant show in the dome. I haven’t been but I hear it’s worth it. The grounds are absolutely beautiful and tranquil, a perfect place for a picnic or a lazy, book-reading afternoon.

This is a great place to visit with the kids. They’ll love it and so will you.
review_2_text: We went with our daughter for a projection on a Thursday morning. Being Semana Santa, it was full but the fact that we can buy tickets online helped a lot, we had no issues. They were well prepared and organized once inside. Besides the projection, the visit is free but the open space is very limited, I would have loved to see more things once inside.
review_3_text:
The Planetario de Madrid is a fantastic spot for both kids and adults who are curious about space and science. The exhibitions are engaging and educational, with interactive displays that make learning fun. It’s well-organized and offers content in both Spanish and English, which is great for international visitors.

The highlight for me was the dome projection shows — truly immersive and visually stunning. Sitting back and watching the stars unfold above you feels magical. The narrations are informative without being too technical, and they do a great job of sparking curiosity about the universe.

Located in a peaceful park area, the Planetario makes for a great half-day visit. It’s easy to reach by public transport, and there’s a nice green space outside for a walk or picnic afterwards. Whether you’re a science enthusiast or just looking for something different to do in Madrid, this place is definitely worth checking out.

description: Polar & jungle ecosystems in a zoo with hundreds of animal species kept in their natural habitats.
address: Av. de las Comunidades, 28, Vicálvaro, 28032 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text: The animals were amazing, but the bubble tea (pre purchased with tickets) they had none... Then they made some (10mins wait) then there was mold floating around in at least two of our 4 drinks. We paid for parking then we got to the gate and the machine ate the ticket and didn't open so we were stuck at the gate! But the animals were great!
review_2_text:
Faunia provides a very well-designed and enjoyable day out. One of the things I appreciated most was the park's layout. There is a single, clear path that guides you through all the different ecosystems, so it's incredibly easy to navigate and you don't have to worry about missing anything or backtracking. It makes for a very relaxing and logical visit. A huge highlight is the sea lion show – it's highly entertaining and definitely a must-see.

​A couple of important tips for future visitors: first, make sure to buy your tickets online in advance. The price at the entrance is significantly higher, so booking ahead can save you a lot of money. Second, it's good to know that this isn't a traditional zoo. You won't find some of the larger animals like lions or giraffes. I'd recommend checking the list of animals on their website beforehand to set the right expectations and avoid any disappointment.
review_3_text: Great experience with several animals shows - be sure to look them in advance so you know exactly when they take place

address: Pl. Mayor, Centro, 28012 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text: Plaza Mayor is Madrid’s historic heart, built in the 17th century under Habsburg rule. Its grand rectangular plan, uniform façades, arcaded walkways, and red-brick architecture frame centuries of markets, coronations, festivals, and public life, reflecting Spain’s imperial power and urban order through symmetry, scale, and ceremonial spatial design traditions enduring.
review_2_text: It is a privilege to be able to walk through and experience the Plaza Mayor while visiting Madrid. The historic square is free to enter and walk around. It is cool to think back to how the plaza would have looked hundreds of years ago, and interesting to think how it will look hundreds of years into the future.
review_3_text: Christmas night at Plaza Mayor was absolutely breathtaking. The towering Christmas tree left me in awe, while the lights adorning the surrounding buildings created a magical glow throughout the plaza. The Christmas market added to the charm, full of life, color, and festive energy. An unforgettable holiday experience in Madrid.

address: Retiro, 28009 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text:
El Retiro Park is one of Madrid’s most beautiful and historic green spaces, stretching across more than 125 hectares and filled with over 15,000 trees. It sits right in the heart of the city and offers a calm escape from the busy streets.

It’s a place where locals and visitors come to walk, relax, and enjoy the outdoors. You can wander through the rose garden, sit by the water, or explore its monuments.
review_2_text: It is probably the best park in Madrid do you cannot miss it! If you like running, relaxing, planning on a picnic or have contact with nature, this is the place to go to. There are so many trees and birds. There are a lot of pets that you can walk through and just go for a nice afternoon stroll. You may also visit with your children or your dogs. Even though it was Winter, I absolutely adored this place. so I cannot imaginé what it is like during Spring or Summer time. There's a lake inside and you can rent pedal boats to relax and observe the beauty. The park is so big that it has many gates to enter!
review_3_text: Such an amazing place to walk around and hang out. The lake is beautiful and easy to sit by for hours. The Palacio de Cristal was closed at the time of the visit, but it still looks amazing from the outside. This place is a must visit. It’s easy to forget being in the middle of a big city.

I had so much fun sport climbing in Madrid! Booking with Gabriel was super easy — I just told him about my climbing experience, how many days I wanted to climb, and the areas I hoped to visit. Everything was smooth and well organized.

My guide Pepe was amazing — kind, encouraging, and such a good coach. He gave great tips, made sure I felt safe, and pushed me just enough to try climbs I didn’t think I could do. I learned a lot and had the best time.

We climbed for three days at La Pedriza, Patones, and San Martín, and every day was an adventure with stunning views and great routes.

Highly recommend this company if you’re looking for a fun, safe, and unforgettable climbing experience in Madrid. I’m already planning my next trip back!

description: Butterflies, flower beds & exhibitions of textile art & nature photography in university gardens.
address: Av. Complutense, s/n, Moncloa - Aravaca, 28040 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text:
I recently went to see Amos Lee and Beth Gibbons at Noches del Botánico, and I was really pleasantly surprised. I hadn’t been to a concert at this venue in years. It was already a lovely venue, and now the space has grown into something truly beautiful. It’s set inside a botanical garden, which makes for a lovely place to wander before the show — peaceful paths, lots of greenery, and quiet corners to sit and relax.

There are also a bunch of vendors selling alternative/hippie-style clothing, jewelry, and some decent food and drinks. The whole vibe is super laid-back but well organized.

We were in the Pista section (the standing area), and it was easy to get right up to the front — such an incredible experience being that close to the artists. Behind the standing area, there’s a seated section for those who prefer to hang back a bit. The concert space itself is just the right size — big enough to feel like a proper live show, but still intimate enough to feel connected to the music and the crowd.

Bathrooms were clean and there were plenty of them (always a plus at outdoor events).

Overall, it was a fantastic night. I’m already looking forward to catching more concerts here — they bring in artists from all over the world every summer, and the setting really makes it special.
review_2_text: Usual Christmas rip off. Once you are inside there are additional charges for various parts of the attraction, ridiculously long queues and generally the only slightly saving grace are some of the lights. Add the „no photos” but yes to videos nonsense and this is best avoided. Much better options in and around Madrid. Such a contrast to the summer concerts here. The park must do better
review_3_text:
What a truly wonderful garden! I was here for around three hours taking endless photos. My legs were sore after walking that long 😂

All the sights were incredible. They have different types of flowers from all over the world. There was a very huge tree, I think the biggest in the whole garden that was amazing to look at. 🌳

My favorite part was the greenhouse. It felt like a totally different world. What a fantastic experience! Each greenhouse had different inner climates and temperatures suited to the different plants inside the greenhouse. Towards the end, you can even climb the stairs to see from above. 🌺 🌼

The cactuses, the mini trees, and the pool by the house with the ducks were also super cool to look at. 🌵

What a captivating, lovely garden in the heart of Madrid. Next time I go to Spain, I will bring my family here. 👍

Four years ago, I arrived in Alapar with my granddaughter, completely unaware of what awaited us: a warm welcome, wonderfully compassionate professionals who made us feel right at home. Clara's progress has been remarkable; she is now able to cope with her difficult situation and has a renewed determination to move forward.

It seems like a miracle that she can now fly. She feels that, even though she's away from her "nest," she can count on the support she needs through Independent Living and the employment office within the Foundation.
My gratitude, as an octogenarian grandmother, for all the help I've received during so many difficult times. Marta has always been unconditionally there for me to help me through. She has become part of my family.
May blessings fall upon them all.

review_1_text: So cute and fun! I only wish it was longer, but definitely worth it, especially if you have young kids!
review_2_text:
The spirit of Christmas once again fills the Royal Post House, located at Calle de Carretas, 4, next to the Sol metro station, from December 5th to January 6th, 2025, hosting the "Forest of Wishes" event.

As in previous years, visitors can write their wishes on paper balls and hang them on the branches of a dozen artificial fir trees. (Bring your own pen, as they are not provided.)

To make the wait more enjoyable, friendly elves inside will assist children in writing their wishes and hanging them on the Christmas trees, along with their best wishes for the coming year.

This activity will be available from December 5th to January 6th. From December 6th to January 4th, the opening hours are from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM. On December 24th and 31st, and January 5th, the opening hours are from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. The event will be closed on December 25th and January 1st.

Admission is free.
review_3_text: It's an excellent idea to leave on the trees in the Forest of Wishes what we wish for our family, the world, and ourselves. Much health and happiness for all. And an end to the war in Ukraine, a wish often repeated on the different trees. I hope it comes true soon. A highly recommended experience, after visiting the Community of Madrid Nativity Scene. It's right next door.

review_1_text:
It couldn't have been a better experience, both for me and the children.
From the moment you walk in, they strive to immerse you in a world of wonder and laughter, no matter your age.
The journey is magical; there are face painters offering a multitude of beautiful designs for both children and adults, balloon twisting that leaves you wondering how they can create that jellyfish with just three balloons... and the best part is the magic shows, where performers come and go, doing "magic tricks for kids" that leave us parents speechless, not knowing what just happened.

But then they add a touch of humor with all the songs and the wonderful camel Manolo, who escapes and causes quite a stir!
It's definitely a trip I plan to repeat and will recommend many times. Happy New Year!
review_2_text:
From the moment we arrived at the station to enjoy this Three Kings' Train ride, we had a wonderful time with all the Kings' helpers, the Magi, and even a sneaky Santa Claus!
We searched for one of the lost camels, created unique Christmas carols, and witnessed close-up magic for all ages.
The Kings' helpers were absolutely fantastic, making this a truly beautiful journey for the whole family.
Thank you all for such a wonderful day! We'll be back next year!
review_3_text:
A huge embarrassment and a massive disappointment.
They advertise it as the Three Kings Train, a special, vintage Christmas train full of magic and fun for children. Nothing could be further from the truth.
Upon arrival, we were put in a compartment with eight other people (there were 12 of us in total). As soon as the train started moving, a "page" appeared, dressed in shabby and tasteless clothing, completely unfunny, with a limited vocabulary and barely speaking during the entire journey, leaving much to be desired.
Then a supposed magician appeared, also poorly dressed and looking unhygienic, who performed for barely two minutes before leaving. For most of the trip, we were left completely alone and bored in the compartment. It was incredibly tedious for the children and exasperating for the parents.
Later, they put on a pathetic show with a man dressed as a camel, looking very cheap, who "escaped" from the train. Next, a face painter appeared, completely unfunny and unprofessional; anyone without experience could have done a better job. The materials gave a very poor impression in terms of hygiene: they were using a dirty, low-quality wet wipe, and as a result, my daughter had a reaction on her face.
Then another person arrived, introducing themselves as a balloon-twisting expert, but they could hardly do anything. All the parents were stunned.
To top it all off, at the end of the route, the Three Wise Men appeared, poorly dressed, badly made up, and with a completely inappropriate way of speaking. Anything but elegant, wise, and majestic. Each child was given a marshmallow and a few sweets, and that was it. Anyone who wanted a photo had to wait in a very long line to get off; those who didn't, left with a balloon.
There was no decoration, no music, no entertainment of any kind. The final impression was one of utter cheapness and of having been ripped off. I absolutely do not recommend it.

address: C. de Felipe IV, Retiro, 28014 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text: Expectacular
review_2_text:
The narration and guide were very entertaining. It gets a bit chilly at night in Madrid, so I recommend dressing warmly. We bought regular tickets and got good seats, but you should arrive at least 30 minutes early.

It's lovely to feel so close to the Christmas lights and magic.
review_3_text:
The bus moves slowly through the brightly lit streets of my Madrid ♥️, and suddenly you realize there are two types of passengers: those who compulsively take photos trying to capture the perfect light (spoiler: you can never get it from the window 😂📸) and those who get carried away and end up with a sore neck from looking up so much. I love the top deck, bundled up 😉

That said, the experience is pure Christmas spirit. You pass through streets that look like they're straight out of a Netflix movie with Christmas carols playing in the background (although it's actually the honking of an impatient taxi 🎶🚕).

In short:
• Ideal for getting into "happy elf" mode. 🧝‍♂️
• If you're with kids, amazing 🤩
• If you're with a partner, romantic.
• If you go with friends... you'll end up belting out Christmas carols.

👉 Practical advice: dress warmly, have your phone charged, and book as soon as the 🎟️ tickets go on sale, because places fly by faster than Santa's reindeer. 🦌🎅

description: Small, child-friendly museum with drawings, figures & recreated home of cartoon mouse & family.
address: C. del Arenal, 8, primera planta, Centro, 28013 Madrid, Spain
short_address: C. del Arenal, 8, primera planta, Madrid
review_1_text:
It’s an interesting idea, but there’s definitely some improvements that could be made.
1) In booking the tour, you’re obliged to have a back-and-forward WhatsApp exchange with an employee about the time. Surely this could be automated? Similarly, the tone of the WhatsApp messages is very patronising- they treat you like you lack basic comprehension skills e.g. “Be advised that the museum is in MADRID.” Yes. We know.
2) The staff member was clearly bored and uninterested and going through the motions. The tour started 10 minutes late (the bossy WhatsApp messages insisted we MUST be early), and, upon noticing that I had an accent, the staff member told me “The tour is in Spanish. It’s not in English.” I told her (in Spanish) that understood that, and that I can understand Spanish. I considered the conversation closed, but at the end of the tour, she again insisted on addressing me in English in front of everyone. I found this impolite, and bad customer service. In addition, she had a bored expression on her face the whole time, which was hardly fun for the kids.
3) The rooms are full of interesting things, but you don’t get given enough time to look at them. Instead, you’re rushed from room to room.
review_2_text: Our visit was great, we love it! The storyteller was excellent just a little fast telling the story, but we still had a very good experience our little one enjoyed it. And they don't let you take pictures or videos.
review_3_text: Beautiful place and loving staff.very magical for the little ones and the entire family. Highly recommended.

review_1_text: Such a cool museum!! It was free and really interesting. Easy to walk around without a guide but I loved the guide we had
review_2_text: I had an absolutely wonderful experience visiting this cultural space in Madrid. The exhibitions were thoughtfully curated and incredibly engaging, showcasing both contemporary art and technology in fascinating ways. The building itself is architecturally stunning with modern design elements throughout. Staff members were friendly and knowledgeable, always ready to help. The interactive displays kept me entertained for hours. Entry was free which made it even better. The location in Gran Via is perfect and easily accessible. I particularly enjoyed the photography exhibition on the fourth floor. This is definitely a must-visit destination for anyone interested in culture, innovation, and creativity. Highly recommended for both locals and tourists!
review_3_text: I visited the museum with my 16-year-old son, who has a strong interest in technology, and we were both impressed. The exhibits showcase the evolution of technology over the years, with fascinating items such as vintage telephones and computer processors from different eras, all presented in an attractive and engaging way. The detailed information panels are clear and well-designed, making the experience both educational and enjoyable. We spent about two hours exploring and reading every display with google translate. The top floor featured beautiful exhibits and well-presented scientific summaries, and the interdisciplinary approach added an extra layer of depth to the visit.

review_1_text:
Parque de Atracciones de Madrid is a fantastic place for a family day out. It’s very easy to reach by metro, which makes the visit stress-free, especially with kids. We spent the whole day there with our two children, and they had an absolutely wonderful time.
There are numerous attractions for all ages — from fun and gentle rides for younger kids to more exciting ones for older children and adults. The park is also surrounded by nature and green areas, which makes it pleasant to walk around and take breaks between rides.
One of the highlights for our kids was seeing different cartoon characters walking around the park. It made the experience even more exciting and interactive for them. There is also a movie theater inside, along with various entertainment options throughout the park.
Overall, it’s a great combination of fun, nature, and entertainment, and we would definitely recommend Parque de Atracciones de Madrid to families visiting the city. 🎢🌿
review_2_text: We had an amazing day at Parque de Atracciones. We used several rides and had a lot of fun, you just need to be prepared for long lines. The park is huge, clean and help is available at every corner.
review_3_text: It's the best amusement park... The rides are amazing and you can enjoy your entire day with your family. For kids and adults there are many places to eat. As it was a Halloween theme, so I loved the scariest halloween party 🥳🥳... The worst part is to wait in the queue for 1 hour to enjoy your favourite rides.

description: Former slaughterhouse housing contemporary arts center hosting exhibitions, performances & cinema.
address: Pl. de Legazpi, 8, Arganzuela, 28045 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐

A beautiful example of industrial recovery! It is impressive to see how these historic slaughterhouse buildings have been transformed into such a striking cultural space. The architecture is stunning, and it is clear there are many well-managed spaces designed for a variety of artistic and community purposes.

However, be mindful of when you visit. We went on a day when no specific events were scheduled, and almost everything was closed except for the main reception area. Without an active exhibition or event, it feels more like a quiet architectural walk rather than a bustling destination.

description: Leisure hub designed as a children's city, with workshops, costumes & games in themed environments.
address: C. de Salvador de Madariaga, S/N, 28702 San Sebastián de los Reyes, Madrid, Spain
short_address: C. de Salvador de Madariaga, S/N, San Sebastián de los Reyes
review_1_text: An amazing way for kids to spend the day playing pretend. Plan ahead though, they want to do every job there is and earn some Eurix while having fun being grown ups. Food is OK and prices are affordable. If you decide to go only afternoon tickets are discounted.
review_2_text:
A Fun and Educational Experience for Kids at Metropolix! Metropolix is an incredible children's park where little ones can explore various professions and have a blast while learning valuable life skills. The staff at Metropolix were exceptionally helpful, nice, kind, and professional, ensuring that every child had a memorable experience.

However, it would be great if the park could also cater to parents' needs. Although they have to purchase tickets, there isn't much for them to do. The seating area should be clean, comfortable, well-lit, and free from noise to create a pleasant atmosphere for adults. Parents deserve to feel as good as their kids do.

Overall, Metropolix provides an exceptional opportunity for children to have fun, learn, and develop essential skills. With a few improvements, it could also become a perfect family outing destination.
review_3_text:
Very disappointing experience. The place was extremely overcrowded, yet they continue to sell tickets. We spent nearly an hour just waiting in line to enter the first activity.

The ticketing system is poorly communicated. Tickets are sold based on age groups, but this isn’t explained clearly. Once inside, you may find that your child isn’t allowed into certain areas because their bracelet (linked to the ticket type) doesn’t grant access. Staff often suggest you go back and buy a more expensive ticket for the next age group, only then do they let you in, but that’s usually after waiting in a long line and you need wait in line again. They don’t seem to consider the actual age or height of the child- taking into account just the ticket price.

Strangely, they also allow small children into attractions meant for older kids (7+), as long as the more expensive ticket has been purchased, which raises serious safety concerns.

To make matters worse, the staff were rude and showed no compassion toward our 3- and 6-year-olds who were visibly upset and crying. There was no patience or understanding, just cold responses and more rules.

description: Amusement park divided into themed areas, with several roller coasters & Batman-inspired rides.
address: A-4, salida 22, 28330 San Martín de la Vega, Madrid, Spain
short_address: A-4, salida 22, San Martín de la Vega
review_1_text:
Visited during the Halloween season and overall had a great time. The park is clean, well themed, and offers a solid mix of rides for both thrill seekers and families. The Gold Fast Pass is absolutely worth buying as it saves hours of waiting and makes the experience far smoother. Food quality was better than expected for a theme park, and the PhotoPass system is a nice way to keep all ride photos digitally.

On the downside, most staff speak very limited English and signage can be confusing. The Gold Pass does not work for the horror mazes and we waited about an hour and a half to enter the IT Experience, which lasted around five minutes. Some vending machines also stopped working later in the evening, which was frustrating after a long day of walking. Still, the Halloween theming and atmosphere were great, and with the Gold Pass, the park is worth visiting.
review_2_text: It is a nice amusement park. There are several actions options and the waiting time in the queue varies due to the popularity and the time of the day. The number of the  shopping store and the merchandise options are adequate. I recommend to visit old stores first and decide what you want to take because each store has their own particular theme and focus, such as looney tunes, gotham city, etc.
review_3_text:
Such a fun park!! Great for a day of playing around, the lines aren’t that big and there’s some rides that are so crazy immersive that you leave fully wondering what was happening! We went in the end of September and already got a lot of cool Halloween experiences and decor!
My main complaints:
- there’s is basically no vegetarian food options inside of the park (im not even gonna mention vegan) Being that you cannot enter with food this is extremely unfortunate. Had to eat a pizza margarita after 30 minutes of searching for food;
- the Halloween experiences were extra charge and it wasn’t that cheap! You pay 44€ for the general admission and then you have to pay an extra 7€ per ride (single). I felt like it wasn’t fair
- there’s more stores and restaurants than rides. The rides are some of the best I’ve ever been on and so much fun, but you have to walk a lot to get to them as you always have to go through stores and restaurants
- more than once was a ride in maintenance while we were there. Eventually we managed to get on all of them but it was annoying to keep having to go back in line

business_name: La Gran Escuela de Magia "Ana Tamariz"
address: C. del Tutor, 25, Moncloa - Aravaca, 28008 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text:
I had the opportunity to take part in the Spanish Immersion Course run by Ana Tamariz at her school in Madrid. Five days of lectures, workshops, shows, and getting to know a group of highly motivated magicians along with award winning Spanish performers, led by the incomparable Juan Tamariz.

Truly a wonderful experience, made all the more enjoyable by the fantastic staff at the Escuela. If memories are made by extraordinary events, then this course has given me a boatload of new ones.

Gracias to all, and I can't wait until we meet again :)
review_2_text:
Little more needs to be said about a magic school that has been teaching magic for 35 years and has produced some of the best magicians in Spain.

But what can be said about the Street Magic course taught by David Navares, with the extraordinary organization of Ana Tamariz, is truly remarkable. In short, it can be summed up in one word: "EXTRAORDINARY." Every magician should take this course if they want to learn the intricacies of the medium and have the skills to serve them well in the future.

10 for David Navares
10 for the great Ana Tamariz School
10 for the organization
10 for the knowledge and theory provided
10 for the practical exercises
10 for the kindness and expertise
10 for the dedication (10 hours scheduled, but we were there for 14)
Do you think I enjoyed it? And the school?

❤️

review_1_text: This was far from the level of service expected. My daughter (under 18) was placed with a host family who left her alone for 3 nights. We informed EF in advance but they didn't take any action.
review_2_text: Bristol was an incredible experience. I watched the sunset from the Clifton Suspension Bridge, explored Banksy’s murals, and visited the M Shed to learn about the city’s history. I loved the harbourside, tried local food at markets, and took a boat ride on the River Avon. What stood out most was discovering hidden bookshops, street art, and the city’s creative, welcoming vibe. It’s a place I’ll always remember.
review_3_text:
If someone told me two years ago that I would be living this experience that I am living right now, I would not believe it.
I’ve wanted to go on an exchange year since I was in primary school and last year my parents and me started looking for different agencies but as soon as we find out EF, we stopped looking for more companies.
Before my exchange year, EF did webinars every week were they told us lots of tips for our exchange years, meetings in different places in the country where I met many people and we did many fun and interactive activities, and they even did some lessons to learn some colloquial language useful for our daily lives that we don’t learn in school. If I had any questions, EF was always available and answered me very quickly and I could feel their support in every step.
During my exchange, my Student Success Advisor was in contact with me the hole time to help me solve every little problem. EF kept doing lots of meetings and activities all around Ireland to know some more places that I wouldn’t have seen if it wasn’t for them and I had the opportunity to make countless friends and met amazing people from different countries all around the world.
I’m staying with a host family and I can’t be more grateful, they take care of me and they count me as another family member, and everything is thanks to EF. This agency is not like the rest of agencies, it makes you feel supported from the beginning to the end of the journey, they accompany you throughout all the way, they help you every time you have a problem, and they not only worry that you are always well and happy, they also make sure that you live the best year of your life.
I definitely recommend this experience to EVERYONE, especially with EF because trusting them, you make sure that each step you take will be unique and unforgettable.

review_1_text:
We celebrated Ian's birthday at La Molona Kids and it was a fantastic choice. From the very first moment, the service was excellent. Sara was always incredibly friendly, explaining each activity in detail and helping us choose the best option for the party, which was much appreciated.

We chose the activity combo, which included a scavenger hunt, a paint fight, and a spectacular finale with a foam cannon, which the children loved. We also did two separate activities: slime making and crafting a wand, which added a fun and original touch to the birthday celebration. Although the price per child was a little higher, it was totally worth it.

We also opted for the hot dog cart, which was great and a big hit with the kids. We loved that you can personalize each birthday party and choose your preferred theme; in our case, it was dinosaurs, and everything looked beautiful.

The children had an amazing time, laughed a lot, and enjoyed themselves from beginning to end. The service throughout the entire party was impeccable.

A very well-organized and unique venue, perfect for celebrating a special birthday. Highly recommended, we would definitely return.
review_2_text:
⭐ An unforgettable birthday thanks to La Molona Kids! ⭐
Yesterday we celebrated my 7-year-old daughter's birthday, and it couldn't have gone better. They came to our common room and had everything ready in no time. It was a completely different birthday party than the typical ball pits, and much cooler.

They set up tons of fun activities: slime, fizzy drawings, and, above all, I want to highlight the paint fight. Plus, everything was done under black light, which made the experience even more amazing for the kids.

I want to give a special mention to Diego, the instructor: patient, friendly, and you can tell he enjoys his work. He won the children over right away, and they had a fantastic time with him.

I also want to thank Sara, who gave me great advice on choosing the activities and helped me with all my questions.

And an extra point: all the products were non-toxic, something I really appreciate.

And I'm also grateful that, despite everything, the room was spotless when we finished.

In short: a perfect 10 experience. We'll definitely be back. Thank you, Molona Kids, for making it so special! 🎉💖
review_3_text:
I contacted Sara to celebrate my son's birthday, and it was the best decision I could have made.
From the very first moment, Sara was incredibly attentive, caring, and made sure everything went smoothly. She offered advice throughout the entire process.
The day arrived… 17 seven-year-olds! It was crazy! And Diego made sure they enjoyed themselves like the kids they are. Every activity was super fun, but the paint fight was a guaranteed hit.
Everyone left happy!!!

A perfect birthday party!!

Thank you, Sara and Diego.

review_1_text: I just called and an old man answered, saying he didn't know what information I was asking for regarding what they offer. They should hire young, qualified people, not elderly people who should be retired and at home.
review_2_text: They don't deserve five stars; they say they'll call you back and then they never do. They're not committed.
review_3_text:
Edit:
After several problems, I decided to look for alternatives, and I've come out on top.
The little one has noticeably improved his skills and is truly learning to skate, with people who are not only true skating professionals but also know how to work with children.
Plus, if there are days when the weather isn't good, they teach him how to do it so he can keep going.

In art, he found a good teacher who knew how to motivate and teach him.
This wasn't the case with skating. After several incidents, the last one exhausting our patience, we decided to terminate him.

The worst part is that we spoke with the head of coordination, and he excused his staff by saying it was a misunderstanding on the part of the little one.

Please, gentlemen, take the time to explain to your staff that there are children who have insecurities and/or difficulty keeping up, because not everyone goes equally fast and not everyone learns at the same pace. Teaching involves taking the time to help those who have more difficulty.

And if you don't know how, can't, or don't want to, find someone truly qualified.
No one pays to have their children denigrated by people who don't know how to teach.

And if you're going to answer, remember one thing:
My little one was humiliated without his worker doing anything to prevent it, and she only came to the exit door to give me excuses. For a child to come to school and be humiliated, and for the person in charge to do NOTHING to prevent it, except to say I don't know what happened, when working with children, is unacceptable.

review_1_text: Excellent Nursery school!👏🏼 Highly recommended.🙌🏼
review_2_text:
Good morning,
The Enchanted Forest is a home. A team that loves the little ones as if they were their own family. This was our first experience with a preschool, as we've been taking our little Sofía there since she was five months old.
Ester, in particular, is sweet, energetic, and friendly. She's very attentive to detail, noticing what each baby needs, and very empathetic in helping the little ones get used to routines like eating, etc. She also gave great advice; I still have the daily summaries on the app—they were like poems! My little one, who was there for 10 months, remembers you so well, and so do we! Paula also exudes calm and patience, telling us what they had done in the afternoon. And the Coordinator, Leticia, and the Director, Marta, are very professional and organized. I remember the day of the power outage; they were all there stoically at their posts waiting for us parents to arrive (they have families too...). They were always available for any questions or concerns. In short, they're all a fantastic team, always smiling and incredibly well-prepared to care for the little ones.

The food is fabulous, and the cook is exceptional. The facilities are spotless, with everything you need and more. Their app is very comprehensive and works perfectly; they keep you updated on everything—whether the children enjoyed their meal, if they were restless, the good and the bad—transparent communication that gives parents peace of mind.

Thank you for your work, Bosque Encantado! You are exceptional!

Finally, my sincere gratitude and appreciation to all public and private nurseries and the professionals who work in them for the essential role they play in our society.
review_3_author: María Yolenny Mena de la Cruz
review_3_text:
We are delighted with the school. Our son has spent two years growing up under the care of professional, kind, and friendly people. From the very beginning, despite the insecurity that first-time parents feel about leaving their little one there, they instilled in us the security, familiarity, and closeness that every parent appreciates.

They keep you involved in your child's development every step of the way, maintain a close relationship with the families, and keep you in constant communication.

The love, the methodology, the attention to detail, and the dedication they put into every activity they do with the children, and most importantly, the fact that our little one has been very happy throughout his time there, have been truly remarkable.

We are incredibly grateful to the entire team at El Bosque Encantado, and especially to the teachers and "second moms" our little one has had, Eva and Laura.

We highly recommend the school.

review_1_text: The swimming pool is too crowded into the mornings because the staff often close off three of the six lanes for lessons, leaving those of is no on lessons with 10 people in a lane at times . I understand that there are some bad reviews but I've had absolutely nice experience.
It is a bit crowded but what can you expect from cheap and nice pool in the center of Madrid?

Ticket for ten entrances will cost you 40€ (there is a discount for younger people) and you can use it in SPECIFIC TIMES (I've uploaded them).
There is also an area near pool for stretching, with instructions :)
review_3_text:
The facilities at the sports center are generally very good. The swimming pool and spa area are excellent, and the cleanliness is usually well maintained. However, depending on the time of day, it can be quite difficult to access the swimming lanes due to high attendance.

In my case, I use the pool as part of a rehabilitation process, and I must say it has been extremely helpful for my recovery. I truly appreciate having access to such a valuable resource.

That said, I feel it is necessary to raise a concern regarding one of the cleaning staff members—a lady with short, curly hair—who has unfortunately displayed a consistently unkind and unhelpful attitude. During her shifts, she often appears to be in a bad mood and shows little willingness to assist when approached. On several occasions, she has simply disappeared when help was requested.

I believe it is important to recognize that many of us attend this center for health and rehabilitation purposes, and respectful, empathetic treatment from staff members is essential.

address: C. de Gaztambide, 29, Chamberí, 28015 Madrid, Spain
review_1_text:
I had the opportunity to collaborate at this center for a while and was able to see firsthand how it operates. It's a place with very complete and well-maintained facilities, with spacious and modern areas. I was particularly impressed by the use of digital tools and software that facilitate both the team's work and the development of the various master's programs and courses. It's focused on digital design.

I also noticed that they have a large number of students, both in-person and online, from different countries and age groups, which creates a very diverse and pleasant environment. There are several well-equipped classrooms and a studio where different activities take place. Overall, the atmosphere is very welcoming, and it's clear that those who work and study there feel comfortable. It's well-located. It has two floors; the rest of the classrooms and the studio are on level -1. There is no parking. You can get information about the educational center at the reception desk.
review_2_text: I was fortunate enough to complete the UX/UI Master's program at TRAZOS in 2025 and was completely satisfied. The facilities, the teaching methods, the flexibility with each student's situation (in-person, online, and recorded classes for review), the structure of the content, the activities, and above all, the incredibly attentive and professional approach of my professor, Jesús Palacios González, are things I will always remember with immense gratitude. I have absolutely no doubt that the investment I made in my training was the right one.
review_3_text:
I studied a double major in Graphic Design at Trazos and I'm incredibly happy with the training. It's a very practical school, focused on exactly what it takes to develop and find work in this sector.
I was lucky to have Rafa Quílez as my Art Direction and Product Design professor, and I can say he's one of the best professors I've ever had, and without a doubt the best professor I had at Trazos.
Rafa truly cares about his students' future, always ensuring we can grow professionally, improve our portfolios, and find work. He truly encouraged us to build a solid portfolio, dedicating time to helping us perfect it, recommending studios to apply to, and constantly pushing us to be the best creatives we can be.
Thanks to him, I feel much more prepared and confident to face the world of work. I really want to return to Trazos just to take more classes with him.

review_1_text:
I went to Implika to receive information about a specific course. Finally I decided not to register. For a personal reason and not because of price or length of the course. That seemed to be ok for the content of that course.

I would like to send a special thank you to Veronica Touceda. She did a great job informing me clearly about every aspect of the course and in a very pleasant and professional way. Also the follow-up from her side was excellent.
review_2_text: I studied ACV here. In the classes, there were people studying their own material, each with their own course. There was one "teacher," if you can even call her that, answering questions (looking at the book just like you and not being able to explain things properly). It wasn't like having in-person classes at all. It was like going to the library to study on your own. Then the exams were pretty pathetic, just true/false questions, and only the last exam required you to write your own explanation. You ask them to tell you your exam grade and see it, and they can't find it; it's been lost (more than one exam). They say it's fine, they'll give you a 9 like your other one, and that's it. I ask them when my course ends, if it's August 1st or September 1st, so I know when to apply for my internship because you have to sign up and apply beforehand. They say they don't know, that they'll tell me, but they never tell me when to apply for my internship. I go to the last exams, and nothing. September 1st arrives, and they tell me to sign up and apply for the internship, and then they realize the deadline has passed. I've been asking you for weeks and you haven't paid me any attention. And I ended up without an internship because they don't care. They just give me the diploma and that's it. (It's a theoretical qualification). 2800 euros down the drain!!! (Even with a discount for having just finished secondary school). I had to get an online stroke certificate elsewhere to be able to do an internship (it cost me 300). And that's what I did. It's the same thing! Don't study there! They're scammers and incompetent! It's not in-person, nobody teaches you anything!!! Oh, and on top of that, I took it to the employment office to look for work in that field, and they told me it's not accredited by the Community of Madrid! Basically, it's useless!! It's not valid! Nobody has hired me, nor have they put me on the job placement list they promise!!!
review_3_text: SCAMMERS!! I'm giving one star because I can't give less. I completed my vocational training in early childhood education at Implika in 2015. My parents paid over €3,000 for me to take classes there and get my qualification. They gave me two binders and promised me classes, but it turns out they put you in a classroom with a bunch of people, each studying their own thing, like a library. There's a teacher who isn't specialized in any of the courses, supposedly there to answer questions, which he sometimes can't even answer. They tell you that a large percentage of students get jobs at the company where you do your internship—ANOTHER LIE. I only had a one-month internship, and they don't hire you. To top it all off, their qualifications are UNOFFICIAL. Someone from this awful company, please explain to me what I'm supposed to do with an unofficial qualification and where I can work with such a worthless degree. When you call and ask for explanations, they tell you that you signed it in the contract, but they know, and you know perfectly well, that when they explain things to you, that's not what they tell you. They promise you a job placement service, which in my opinion is a lie since there are hardly any job openings and they're always the same. NO ONE SHOULD STUDY HERE. THEY'RE DISGUSTING.

review_1_text:
This review is for Algorithmics in Valdebebas. My son took a two-year Python Introductory course (ages 13-14). I have advanced programming skills, but I thought it was better to have someone outside the family take charge of the classes. From the beginning, strange things happened. I asked to be told where the weekly lessons were on the platform, so I could review exercises with the son, and it turned out that "the teacher was very experienced but didn't follow the platform" (something advertised on the website as a strong point of the courses). I had a brief chat with that teacher and decided to trust him.
In two years, he's had at least three teachers, none of whom followed the platform, and I've had only one quarterly evaluation in the two years. Once the two years were over, I reviewed the lessons with the son this summer to see how he was doing, and I discovered he barely knows anything; he gets confused with basic concepts he should have mastered in the first year. I've asked them for evaluations from the past two years and student follow-up reports, insisting several times for a month, letting them know that I had yet to write this review, and they haven't provided or explained anything at all.

I feel completely scammed. I hope this review helps someone else consider the level of trust they should place in this company.
review_2_text: I posted about some windows I paid for with built-in screens. They're washing their hands of it like Pontius Pilate and refusing to take responsibility for the lack of instructions. They installed them haphazardly, and as expected, they didn't do a good job, and they're completely ignoring the issue. The customer service phone line never works, and you have to go there in person just to get excuses for everything. Now my windows, which I would have replaced with blinds if the screens hadn't been included, have poorly installed screens.
review_3_text: Very good school with excellent professionals. Highly recommended.
